Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Misassigning tissues; trap: Kidney from endoderm (actually mesoderm) Confusing primitive vs definitive layers; mistake: Primitive endoderm as definitive Ignoring mesoderm’s broad role; trap: Questions on blood vessels (mesoderm)
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks Ecto: External (Skin/Eyes/Nerves) Meso: Middle (Muscles/Bones/Blood) Endo: Internal (Guts/Glands/Lungs)
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)List tissues from mesoderm.
All muscles, dermis of skin, connective tissue, bone, cartilage, blood vessels, kidneys, reproductive organs.
What is the role of the node in germ layer formation?
Anterior-most tip of primitive streak, functions as organizing center for morphogenic gradients.
Topic 4 – Epithelial-Mesenchymal Transition (EMT)Big Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)EMT/MET involves cellular changes in division, shape, migration, growth, and secretion for tissue morphogenesis.Mind map: EMT → Loss of Cell-Cell Adhesion → Gain Cell-ECM Adhesion → Polarity Change → Motility/Invasion → Examples (Condensation/Shape Change) Must-Know Facts (max 12 bullet points) Epithelial: Sheets/tubes, tightly connected, polarity 🔴 Mesenchymal: Unconnected, independent, no polarity 🟢 EMT accompanies morphogenesis with changes in division direction/number 🔵 Cell shape changes (e.g., cobblestone to acini in MCF-10A) 🟢 Cell migration/movement 🔴 Cell growth/death 🟢 Secretion changes 🔵 Example: Vascular mesenchymal cell aggregation (condensation) 🔴 MCF-10A on Matrigel forms acini 🟢 Lumen formation via cell death in acini 🔴 Polarity dependent on cadherins 🟢 Occurs in wound healing for alignment 🔵
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)EMT Process:Epithelial (Polar, Tight) --> Transition (Shape/Migration Changes) --> Mesenchymal (Loose, Mobile)Reverse: MET
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Process EMT (X) MET (Y)
Change Epithelial to mesenchymal Mesenchymal to epithelial
Adhesion Lose cell-cell, gain cell-ECM Gain cell-cell, lose cell-ECM
Polarity Lose apical-basal Gain apical-basal
Example Wound healing invasion Tissue stabilization
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Confusing EMT with apoptosis; trap: Lumen formation questions (via death, not just hollowing) Overlooking examples; mistake: Assuming all cells undergo EMT equally Misidentifying polarity role; trap: Cadherin dependency in alignment
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks EMT: Every Migration Transforms (Division/Shape/Movement/Growth/Secretion) 5 Changes: Di-Sha-Mi-Gro-Se (Direction/Shape/Migration/Growth/Secretion)
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Describe EMT changes.
Direction/number of divisions; 2. Shape changes; 3. Movement/migration; 4. Growth/death; 5. Membrane/secretion changes.
Explain MCF-10A example.
At confluence, cobblestone; in 3D Matrigel, proliferate to clusters then acini with polarized outer layer and inner cell death for lumen.
Topic 5 – Blood Vessel DevelopmentBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Blood vessels form via vasculogenesis and angiogenesis to meet embryonic needs by third week.Mind map: Vasculogenesis (In Situ Endothelial Formation → Network → Stabilization) → Angiogenesis (Sprouting from Existing) → Structure (Adventitia/Media/Intima) → Lumen (Hollowing/Cord) Must-Know Facts (max 12 bullet points) Starts by third gestational week due to diffusion limits 🔴 Two processes: Vasculogenesis (in situ in non-vascularized tissue) 🟢 Angiogenesis (new vessels from existing) 🔵 Vessel walls: Adventitia, media, intima (capillaries thin endothelium) 🟢 Linked to heart development 🔴 Initial network: Veins/arteries + plexus 🟢 Growth factors stabilize walls, recruit pericytes/SMCs 🔵 Blood flow essential for proper development 🔴 Endothelial tube on fibronectin lines (30μm wide) 🟢 Lumen models: Cell hollowing (vacuoles coalesce) or cord hollowing (shape changes) 🔴 Cord hollowing more feasible for vasculogenesis 🟢 Luminal cell death alternative mechanism 🔵
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Vessel Formation:Vasculogenesis --> Endothelial Cells --> Tube --> Mural Cells (Pericytes/SMCs)Lumen: Intracellular Vacuoles --> Coalesce --> Interconnect
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Process Vasculogenesis (X) Angiogenesis (Y)
Location Non-vascularized tissue From existing vessels
Mechanism In situ endothelial formation Sprouting/branching
Stage Early embryo, linked to heart Later growth/remodeling
Outcome Initial network (veins/arteries/plexus) Expanded vasculature
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Confusing vasculogenesis with angiogenesis; trap: Initial vs expansion questions Ignoring flow role; mistake: Assuming vessels develop without hemodynamics Wrong lumen model; trap: Assuming only hollowing (evidence for cord)
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks V-A: Vasculogenesis (Virgin Areas) vs Angiogenesis (Add to Existing) Lumen: Hollow Cords Vacate (Hollowing/Cord/Vacuoles)
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Differentiate vasculogenesis and angiogenesis.
Vasculogenesis: In situ endothelial formation in non-vascularized tissue, initial network; Angiogenesis: New endothelium from existing vessels.
Describe lumen formation models.
Cell hollowing: Vacuoles coalesce and interconnect; Cord hollowing: Packed cells change shape, break junctions for extracellular lumen.
Topic 6 – Branching MorphogenesisBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Branching morphogenesis creates complex structures like lungs via inductive chemicals and inhibitors.Mind map: Traditional Model (FGF10 Induce → Shh/BMP4 Inhibit) → Types (Domain/Orthogonal/Planar) → Reaction-Diffusion (Activator/Inhibitor → Patterns) Must-Know Facts (max 12 bullet points) Traditional: FGF10 inductive, Shh/BMP4/Sprouty inhibit for branching 🔴 Lung types: Domain, orthogonal, planar 🟢 Reaction-diffusion: Turing’s activator-inhibitor system 🔵 Activator: BMP-2 (postulated); Inhibitor: MGP 🟢 Equations involve diffusion, reaction, chemotaxis 🔴 Applications: Pigment distribution, hair follicles 🟢 Domain branching: Low inhibitor consumption 🔵 Orthogonal: High consumption 🔴 Planar: With boundary effect 🟢 Simulation matches lung anatomy 🔵 Alan Turing: Mathematical biology pioneer 🔴 System: Pair activator-inhibitor with cells 🟢
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Reaction-Diffusion Equations:∂u/∂t = D_u ∇²u + γ (nu² / (1 + ku² v) - u + c)∂v/∂t = D_v ∇²v + γ (nu² - v + e)∂n/∂t = D_n ∇²n - ∇ · (χ n ∇u) + r n (1 - n / k_n) + [u / (1 + u)] n
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Type Domain Branching (X) Orthogonal Branching (Y)
Inhibitor Consumption Low High
Morphology Along surface Perpendicular rotation
Simulation Matches early lung Matches mid-lung
Switch N/A To planar with boundary
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Ignoring Turing’s role; trap: History questions on models Misapplying equations; mistake: Forgetting cell term Confusing branch types; trap: Lung anatomy diagrams
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks Branch Types: DOP (Domain-Orthogonal-Planar) Turing: Turing’s Universal Reaction Inhibits Natural Growth (for activator-inhibitor)
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Explain traditional branching model.
FGF10 as inductive chemical; Shh, BMP4, Sprouty inhibit FGF10, leading to localized branching.
Describe reaction-diffusion in branching.
Activator (BMP-2) and inhibitor (MGP) system with cells; diffusion/reaction create patterns like lung branches.
Topic 7 – Left-Right AsymmetryBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Left-right asymmetry arises from cellular chirality and cilia to establish organ sidedness.Mind map: LR Asymmetry → Cilia (Node Flow → Gradient) → Planar Chirality (Epithelium → Rotation) → Examples (Heart/Fiber → Twins/Mirror Imaging) Must-Know Facts (max 12 bullet points) Prominent from microscopic to macroscopic levels 🔴 Cilia at node generate leftward flow for sidedness 🟢 Flow sets morphogen gradient or activation 🔵 Planar cell polarity orients cilia 🔴 Not solely cilia; early twins show mirror imaging 🟢 Single cell chirality: BDM induces counter-clockwise torsion 🔵 Hindgut rotation driven by epithelial planar chirality 🔴 Vascular mesenchymal cells form chiral structures on patterns 🟢 Skeletal myogenesis: Chiral orientation before fusion 🔵 Myotubes on wide grooves show intense contractions 🔴 Engineering: Actin striation in myotubes 🟢 Twins: Book-ending defects suggest pre-cilia chirality 🔵
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)LR Establishment:Node Cilia --> Leftward Flow --> Morphogen Gradient --> AsymmetryCell Chirality: Counter-Clockwise Torsion --> Organ Sidedness
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Mechanism Cilia Flow (X) Cell Chirality (Y)
Level Multicellular (node) Single cell
Example Embryo-wide asymmetry Zygote torsion, hindgut
Dependency Fluid flow Intrinsic polarity
Evidence Science mag diagrams BDM treatment
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Assuming only cilia; trap: Twin questions (not 100% identical) Overlooking single cell role; mistake: All asymmetry post-gastrulation Confusing torsion direction; trap: BDM timing effects
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks LR: Leftward Rotation (Cilia flow) Chirality: Cells Have Intrinsic Rotation Always Leading Inward (for torsion)
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)How do cilia establish LR asymmetry?
Node cilia generate leftward fluid flow, setting extracellular morphogen gradient or differential activation.
Explain single cell chirality in asymmetry.
BDM treatment post-fertilization induces counter-clockwise torsion in zygotes, contributing to LR before cilia.
Topic 8 – Cell Adhesion MoleculesBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Cell adhesion via CAMs like cadherins, integrins, selectins mediates cell-cell/matrix interactions for tissue formation.Mind map: Adhesion → Families (Cadherins → Integrins → Selectins → Ig CAMs) → Types (Homotypic/Heterotypic) → Functions (Junctions/Signaling/Rolling) Must-Know Facts (max 12 bullet points) Four families: Cadherins (Ca-dependent, cell-cell), Ig CAMs (Ca-independent), Integrins (cell-matrix), Selectins (leukocyte rolling) 🔴 Homotypic: Same molecules; Heterotypic: Different 🟢 Cadherins: E (epithelial), N (neural), P (placenta); Ca-binding domain 🔵 Integrins: αβ heterodimers, 18α/8β, mechanotransducers, bidirectional signaling 🔴 Selectins: L (leukocytes), E (ECs), P (platelets/ECs); Ca-dependent carbohydrate-binding 🟢 Focal adhesions: Concentrated sites via integrins 🔵 In wound healing: Cadherin-dependent polarity 🔴 Leukocyte rolling: Selectin-mediated transient adhesion 🟢 Extravasation: Selectin arrest + integrin adhesion 🔵 Lung-on-chip: Reconstitutes alveolar-capillary interface 🔴 Inflammation: Neutrophil extravasation via cytokines/bacteria 🟢 Cell-cell vs cell-substrate adhesion crucial for engineering 🔵
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Integrin Conformation:Low Affinity (Folded, Clasped Tails) <--> High Affinity (Straight, Separated)Cadherin: Transmembrane --> Extracellular Ca-Binding --> Homotypic Dimer
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Molecule Cadherins (X) Integrins (Y)
Binding Cell-cell, Ca-dependent Cell-matrix, bidirectional
Structure Transmembrane, homotypic αβ heterodimer, tails ~50aa
Examples E-cadherin (epithelium) α1β1 (collagen)
Function Junctions, polarity Focal adhesions, signaling
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Confusing cadherins with integrins; trap: Binding types (cell-cell vs matrix) Forgetting Ca role; mistake: Cadherins work without Ca (dimer fails) Misunderstanding selectin rolling; trap: Permanent vs transient adhesion
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks CAM Families: C-I-S-I (Cadherins-Integrins-Selectins-Ig) Integrin: Inside-Out, Outside-In (Bidirectional signaling)
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Compare cadherins and integrins.
Cadherins: Ca-dependent cell-cell adhesion, homotypic, e.g., E-cadherin; Integrins: Cell-matrix, αβ, mechanotransducers, bidirectional.
Describe selectin-mediated rolling.
Transient adhesion under flow; L-selectin on leukocytes binds PSGL-1 on ECs, leading to arrest then integrin-mediated transmigration.
Topic 9 – Extracellular Matrix (ECM)Big Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)ECM provides structural support and signaling via proteins, GAGs, and glycoproteins surrounding cells.Mind map: ECM → Components (Proteins → GAGs → Glycoproteins) → Basal Lamina (Sheet 60-120nm) → Types (Collagen/Laminin/Elastin/Fibronectin) Must-Know Facts (max 12 bullet points) 3D network of fibers in hydrated gel 🔴 Secreted locally by cells, assembles scaffold for attachment/proliferation 🟢 Cells modify via enzymes/load 🔵 Components: Proteins (collagen/elastin/laminin), GAGs (chondroitin/heparin), Proteoglycans (fibrinogen) 🔴 Basal lamina: 60-120nm sheet, surrounds epithelia, important in repair/embryogenesis 🟢 Basement membrane: Basal lamina + collagen VI + reticular tissue 🔵 Collagen: Main strength, 25-35% body protein, triple helix (Type I: 2α1+1α2) 🔴 Type IV: In basal lamina, 7S/middle/NC1 domains, forms hexamers/tetramers 🟢 Laminin: Networks in basal lamina, binds cells/integrins, for stem cell culture 🔵 Elastin: Elasticity, stretches 5x, interwoven with collagen 🔴 Fibronectin: Binds integrins/collagen/fibrin, soluble (plasma) or insoluble (cellular) 🟢 Wrinkle: Broken elastin replaced by collagen, looser/stiffer skin 🔵
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Collagen Structure:Polypeptide Chains --> Triple Helix --> Fibrils (Heterotypic, e.g., I+VI)Basal Lamina: Laminin Networks + Type IV Collagen + Proteoglycans
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Component Collagen (X) Elastin (Y)
Function Strength, rigidity Elasticity, stretch
Structure Triple helix fibrils Hydrophobic, 750aa
Location Tendons, ligaments Lung, vessels, bladder
In Aging Maintains integrity Degradation causes wrinkles
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Confusing basal lamina with basement; trap: Thickness/composition (lamina 60-120nm, basement includes collagen layer) Misidentifying collagen types; mistake: Type IV as fibrillar (actually network-forming) Overlooking fibronectin types; trap: Soluble vs insoluble roles
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks ECM: Extra Cellular Material (Proteins-GAGs-Glycoproteins) Collagen Types: I Fibril, IV Basal (1-Fiber, 4-Foundation)
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)What is ECM composition?
Proteins (collagen, elastin, laminin), GAGs (chondroitin, heparin), glycoproteins/proteoglycans (fibronectin, fibrinogen).
Describe Type IV collagen structure.
Alpha-chains with 7S, triple-helical, NC1 domains; two assemble to NC1 hexamer, four to 7S tetramer.
Active Recall Bank What is morphogenesis? _____ Define cleavage. _____ Name the 3 germ layers. _____ What does ectoderm form? _____ Describe EMT. _____ Differentiate vasculogenesis from angiogenesis. _____ What is reaction-diffusion mechanism? _____ How do cilia contribute to LR asymmetry? _____ Name 4 CAM families. _____ What is integrin function? _____ Define basal lamina. _____ Main ECM component for strength? _____ Laminin role in basal lamina? _____ Elastin vs collagen in skin? _____ Fibronectin types? _____ Primitive streak formation? _____ Nodal signaling gradient? _____ Example of cell shape change? _____ Lumen formation models? _____ Branching types in lung? _____
15-Minute Mock Exam Explain the importance of morphogenesis in tissue engineering.
It’s a process from natural selection for optimal tissue function; incorporating mechanisms aids repair of non-self-repairing tissues.
Describe gastrulation process.
Establishes 3 germ layers (ecto/endo/meso) via primitive streak in planar embryo with amniotic cavity and yolk sac.
Compare epithelial and mesenchymal cells.
Epithelial: Tightly connected sheets, polar; Mesenchymal: Loose independent units, non-polar.
What are the two lumen formation models?
Cell hollowing (vacuoles coalesce); Cord hollowing (shape changes break junctions).
Explain reaction-diffusion in branching.
Activator-inhibitor pair (e.g., BMP-2/MGP) with diffusion/reaction creates patterns like branches.
How is LR asymmetry established beyond cilia?
Single cell chirality (torsion) and planar chirality (epithelium rotation) contribute, as seen in twins.
Differentiate cadherins and selectins.
Cadherins: Ca-dependent cell-cell junctions; Selectins: Ca-dependent carbohydrate-binding for leukocyte rolling.
What is ECM and its components?
Non-cellular network for support/signaling; proteins (collagen), GAGs, glycoproteins (fibronectin).
One-Page Cheat Sheet Summary
Topic Key Points
Morphogenesis Pattern/body plan/symmetry; Importance: Repair mechanisms
Embryonic Stages Zygote → Cleavage → Morula → Blastocyst → Gastrulation
Germ Layers Ecto: Skin/nerves; Meso: Muscles/bones; Endo: Guts/glands
EMT Epithelial → Mesenchymal: Shape/migration changes
Blood Vessels Vasculogenesis (in situ); Angiogenesis (sprout); Lumen: Hollow/cord
Branching FGF10 induce; Types: Domain/Orthogonal/Planar; Turing RD
LR Asymmetry Cilia flow; Cell chirality: Torsion/rotation
Adhesion Cadherins (cell-cell); Integrins (matrix); Selectins (rolling)
ECM Collagen (strength); Laminin (networks); Elastin (stretch); Fibronectin (binding)

entry.title: BME 2104 Tissue Engineering – 4
entry.content:
BME 2104 Tissue Engineering – 4Lecture Overview Top 8 topics that appear every year: Fibronectin and RGD Binding, Focal Adhesions in 2D vs 3D, Cell Migration and Chemotaxis, Signaling Ligands and Types, Central Paradigm of Signal Transduction, G-Protein Coupled Receptors, Receptor Tyrosine Kinases and Pathways, Mechanotransduction and Cytoskeleton Colour code: 🔴 Memorise | 🟢 Understand | 🔵 Apply 每年必出嘅八大題目 (Top 8 topics that appear every year): Fibronectin 纖連蛋白 同 RGD Binding 結合 Focal Adhesions 黏著斑 in 2D vs 3D Cell Migration 細胞遷移 同 Chemotaxis 趨化性 Signaling Ligands 信號配體 同 Types 類型 Central Paradigm of Signal Transduction 信號轉導中心法則 G-Protein Coupled Receptors (GPCRs) G蛋白偶聯受體 Receptor Tyrosine Kinases (RTKs) 受體酪氨酸激酶 同 Pathways 信號途徑 Mechanotransduction 機械轉導 同 Cytoskeleton 細胞骨架
顏色標記 (Colour code): 🔴 Memorise 必須背誦 | 🟢 Understand 必須理解 | 🔵 Apply 必須應用
Topic 1 – Fibronectin 纖連蛋白 同 Integrin 整合素 Binding 結合大圖景 (Big Picture)Fibronectin 纖連蛋白係關鍵嘅 ECM (Extracellular Matrix) 細胞外基質 glycoprotein 糖蛋白，透過 integrins 整合素促進 cell adhesion 細胞黏附，對組織結構同信號傳遞至關重要。 思維導圖 (Mind map):Fibronectin → Types 類型 (Soluble Plasma 可溶性血漿型 → Bloodstream 血液循環 → Hepatocytes 肝細胞; Insoluble Cellular 不可溶性細胞型 → Secreted by Fibroblasts 成纖維細胞分泌) → Binds to 結合對象 (Integrins 整合素 → RGD Sequence RGD序列 → α5β1/αVβ3; ECM Components ECM成分 → Collagen 膠原蛋白/Fibrin 纖維蛋白/Heparan Sulfate 硫酸肝素) → Enables Cell Attachment 實現細胞黏附。 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) 🔴 Fibronectin 纖連蛋白係結合 integrins 整合素嘅主要 ECM 糖蛋白。 🟢 亦結合其他 ECM 成分，如 collagen 膠原蛋白、fibrin 纖維蛋白、heparan sulfate proteoglycans 硫酸肝素蛋白聚糖。 🔴 兩種類型 (Two types): soluble plasma 可溶性血漿型 (hepatocytes 肝細胞產生) 同 insoluble cellular 不可溶性細胞型 (fibroblasts 成纖維細胞等分泌)。 🟢 Integrin 整合素 binding 結合使用 RGD sequence RGD序列 (Arg-Gly-Asp 精氨酸-甘氨酸-天冬氨酸)。 🔴 關鍵 integrins 整合素: α5β1 同 αVβ3 (喺細胞表面)。 🟢 短嘅 RGD peptides RGD肽可以模仿 fibronectin 纖連蛋白功能，促進 cell attachment 細胞黏附。 🔵 RGD 序列促成 focal adhesion 黏著斑形成。 🟢 Plasma fibronectin 血漿纖連蛋白喺血液中循環；cellular fibronectin 細胞型纖連蛋白則整合入 matrix 基質。 🔴 RGD 係 primary cell attachment site 主要細胞黏附位點。 🟢 Binding 結合促進 clustering 聚集，形成 focal adhesions 黏著斑。 🔵 應用於 biomaterials 生物材料，用於 cell adhesion engineering 細胞黏附工程。 🟢 Insoluble form 不可溶性型由好多細胞分泌，例如 fibroblasts 成纖維細胞。 🔴 Heparan sulfate 硫酸肝素 binding 結合有助於 growth factor 生長因子 localization 定位。 🟢 Fibronectin 纖連蛋白 domains 結構域可以結合 TGF-β 同 bFGF。
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Fibronectin Structure 纖連蛋白結構:NH2 - [Type I Repeats I型重複序列] - [Type II II型] - [Type III III型] - COOHBindings 結合位點: Fibrin纖維蛋白/Heparan硫酸肝素 (I型), Collagen膠原蛋白 (I/II型), Integrin整合素 (III型 - RGD), Heparan硫酸肝素/Fibrin纖維蛋白 (III型) RGD Binding RGD結合:Cell Surface Integrin 細胞表面整合素 (α5β1/αVβ3) ⇄ RGD Sequence RGD序列 → Cell Attachment 細胞黏附
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Soluble Plasma vs Insoluble Cellular Fibronectin 可溶性血漿型 vs 不可溶性細胞型纖連蛋白
Aspect 方面 Soluble Plasma Fibronectin (X) 可溶性血漿型纖連蛋白 Insoluble Cellular Fibronectin (Y) 不可溶性細胞型纖連蛋白
Source 來源 Produced by hepatocytes 肝細胞產生 Secreted by fibroblasts etc. 成纖維細胞等分泌
Location 位置 Circulates in bloodstream 喺血液循環中 Integrated into ECM 整合入細胞外基質
Function 功能 Coagulation 凝血, wound healing 傷口癒合 Cell adhesion 細胞黏附, matrix structure 基質結構
Solubility 溶解性 Soluble 可溶性 Insoluble 不可溶性
Production 生產 Liver-specific 肝臟特異性 Various cell types 多種細胞類型
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 誤以為 RGD 係唯一結合位點 (陷阱: 忘記其他協同區域，例如 PHSRN)。 混淆 fibronectin 纖連蛋白嘅類型: 以為所有纖連蛋白都係 insoluble 不可溶性，喺 ECM 入面。 忽略 RGD 喺 tissue engineering (TE) 組織工程應用中，用於 synthetic peptides 合成肽嘅作用。
記憶法 (Mnemonics / Memory Hooks) RGD: “Really Good Dance” – Arg-Gly-Asp 精氨酸-甘氨酸-天冬氨酸令細胞”跳舞” (黏附同移動)。 Fibronectin types 纖連蛋白類型: “Plasma Soluble in Blood, Cellular Insoluble in Matrix” – PSB-CIM。
過往試題 + 標準答案 (Past Exam Questions + Model Answers)What is the role of the RGD sequence in fibronectin? 纖連蛋白中 RGD 序列嘅作用係咩？
The RGD (Arg-Gly-Asp) sequence is the primary integrin-binding site facilitating cell attachment via α5β1 and αVβ3 integrins.RGD (精氨酸-甘氨酸-天冬氨酸) 序列係主要嘅整合素結合位點，透過 α5β1 同 αVβ3 整合素促進細胞黏附。
Compare soluble and insoluble fibronectin. 比較可溶性同不可溶性纖連蛋白。
Soluble plasma fibronectin circulates in blood from hepatocytes for systemic roles; insoluble cellular fibronectin is secreted locally for ECM integration and adhesion.可溶性血漿型纖連蛋白由肝細胞產生，喺血液中循環，發揮全身性作用；不可溶性細胞型纖連蛋白由局部細胞分泌，用於 ECM 整合同黏附。
Topic 2 – Focal Adhesions 黏著斑大圖景 (Big Picture)Focal adhesions 黏著斑係 integrin 整合素介導嘅聚集結構，連結 ECM 細胞外基質 同 cytoskeleton 細胞骨架，傳遞信號同機械力。思維導圖 (Mind map):Focal Adhesion 黏著斑 → Integrin Binds 整合素結合 (Collagen 膠原蛋白/Fibronectin 纖連蛋白 → RGD) → Cluster Formation 聚集形成 → Components 組成部分 (Vinculin/Talin/FAK/Actin/Myosin) → Signals to Nucleus 信號傳至細胞核 → 2D (Punctate 點狀) vs 3D (Diffuse 瀰漫性). 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) 🔴 Integrins 整合素 bind 結合 ECM (如 collagen 膠原蛋白/fibronectin 纖連蛋白) 形成 focal adhesions 黏著斑。 🟢 Focal adhesions 黏著斑係細胞黏附聚集結構。 🔴 顯微成像中常用綠色螢光標記 focal adhesions 黏著斑。 🟢 2D 環境下嘅 adhesions 黏著斑呈 punctate 點狀；3D 環境下呈 fibrillar/diffuse 纖維狀/瀰漫性。 🔵 透過 cytoskeleton 細胞骨架將 mechanical signals 機械信號傳遞至 nucleus 細胞核。 🟢 涉及蛋白質: vinculin, talin, paxillin, FAK。 🔴 Neural progenitor cells (NPCs) 神經祖細胞: 喺 3D 環境中，remodeling 重塑 > binding 結合。 🟢 NPC stemness NPC幹性對 3D 環境中嘅 adhesive ligands 黏附配體不敏感。 🔵 應用於 TE 組織工程，用於模仿 ECM 嘅 scaffold 支架設計。 🟢 機械力來自 actin-myosin contraction 肌動蛋白-肌球蛋白收縮。 🔴 Turnover 更新調節 migration 遷移。 🟢 FAK 透過 phosphorylation 磷酸化進行信號傳遞。 🔵 3D 環境會改變 adhesion morphology 黏附形態。
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Focal Adhesion Assembly 黏著斑組裝:ECM (Fibronectin)細胞外基質 (纖連蛋白) → Integrin整合素 → Talin/Vinculin → Actin/Myosin肌動蛋白/肌球蛋白 → Force Transmission力傳遞 → Nucleus細胞核 (Gene Expression基因表達) 2D vs 3D:2D: Flat substrate 平坦基底 → Discrete spots 離散點狀3D: Matrix embedding 基質包埋 → Elongated fibrils 延長纖維狀
對比表 (Comparison Table) - 2D vs 3D Focal Adhesions
Aspect 方面 2D Focal Adhesions (X) 3D Focal Adhesions (Y)
Morphology 形態 Punctate 點狀, discrete 離散 Fibrillar 纖維狀, diffuse 瀰漫性
Environment 環境 Flat surfaces 平坦表面 Volumetric matrices 立體基質
Function 功能 Strong anchorage 強力錨定 Dynamic remodeling 動態重塑
Imaging 成像 Bright spots 明亮點狀 Linear extensions 線性延伸
NPC Response 神經祖細胞反應 Ligand-sensitive 配體敏感 Remodeling-focused 聚焦重塑
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 假設 2D 同 3D 嘅 adhesions 黏著斑係一樣嘅 (陷阱: 喺 TE 設計中忽略維度影響)。 忘記 focal adhesions 黏著斑除咗黏附，仲有 signaling 信號傳遞嘅作用。 忽略 NPC 神經祖細胞喺 3D 中對 ligands 配體不敏感呢點。
記憶法 (Mnemonics / Memory Hooks) Focal Adhesion 黏著斑: “FACTS” – Fibronectin-Actin Connection Transmits Signals 纖連蛋白-肌動蛋白連接傳遞信號。 2D vs 3D: “Punctate in Plane (2D), Fibrils in Full (3D)” 平面上點狀(2D)，立體中纖維(3D)。
過往試題 + 標準答案 (Past Exam Questions + Model Answers)Describe focal adhesion formation. 描述黏著斑嘅形成。
Integrins bind ECM (e.g., fibronectin via RGD), clustering with talin/vinculin/actin to form adhesions transmitting signals.整合素結合 ECM (例如，透過 RGD 結合纖連蛋白)，與 talin/vinculin/肌動蛋白聚集形成黏著斑，傳遞信號。
How do focal adhesions differ in 2D vs 3D? 黏著斑喺 2D 同 3D 環境中有咩唔同？
In 2D, they are punctate for strong anchorage; in 3D, fibrillar for remodeling, as seen in NPC maintenance.喺 2D 環境，佢哋呈點狀用於強力錨定；喺 3D 環境，呈纖維狀用於重塑，正如喺 NPC 維持中所見。
(Topic 3-8 及以下部分因篇幅限制，將以同樣模式呈現關鍵詞中英對照，但內容精簡。) Topic 3 – Cell Migration 細胞遷移 同 Chemotaxis 趨化性大圖景 (Big Picture)Cell migration 細胞遷移涉及 cytoskeleton 細胞骨架重組以進行移動，由 taxis 趨向性引導，例如 development 發育同 repair 修復中嘅 chemotaxis 趨化性。Key Terms: Actin polymerization 肌動蛋白聚合, Myosin 肌球蛋白, Haptotaxis 趨觸性, Phototaxis 趨光性, Embryogenesis 胚胎發生。 Topic 4 – Signaling Ligands 信號配體 同 Types 類型大圖景 (Big Picture)Ligands 配體啟動 signaling 信號以協調組織發育，根據類型、作用範圍同功能分類。Key Terms: Diffusible 可擴散, ECM-bound 細胞外基質結合, Endocrine 內分泌, Paracrine 旁分泌, Autocrine 自分泌, Juxtacrine 近分泌/並列分泌, Growth factors (GFs) 生長因子, Cytokines 細胞因子, Chemokines 趨化因子. Topic 5 – Central Paradigm of Signal Transduction 信號轉導中心法則大圖景 (Big Picture)Signal transduction 信號轉導將 extracellular 細胞外 ligands 配體轉化為 intracellular 細胞內 responses 反應，透過 receptors 受體同 cascades 級聯反應，最終導致 gene activation 基因激活。Key Terms: Initiation 啟動, Transduction 轉導, Transcription factors (TFs) 轉錄因子, Second messengers 第二信使, Translation 翻譯, mRNA vaccines mRNA疫苗。 Topic 6 – G-Protein Coupled Receptors (GPCRs) G蛋白偶聯受體大圖景 (Big Picture)GPCRs 透過 G-protein G蛋白解離傳遞多樣化信號，參與 sensory perception 感官知覺同生理調節。Key Terms: 7 transmembrane (7TM) segments 7次跨膜片段, Agonist 激動劑, G-protein subunits (α/β/γ) G蛋白亞基, Effectors 效應器, Adenylate cyclase 腺苷酸環化酶, Phospholipase C (PLC) 磷脂酶C。 Topic 7 – Receptor Tyrosine Kinases (RTKs) 受體酪氨酸激酶 同 Pathways 信號途徑大圖景 (Big Picture)RTKs 喺 ligand 配體結合後 dimerize 二聚化並發生 phosphorylation 磷酸化，啟動 cascades 級聯反應，參與生長同 angiogenesis 血管生成。Key Terms: Dimerization 二聚化, Autophosphorylation 自磷酸化, Ras/Raf/MEK/ERK pathway, TGF-β (Transforming Growth Factor-β) 轉化生長因子-β, Smad proteins Smad蛋白, Wnt pathway, β-catenin β-連環蛋白。 Topic 8 – Mechanotransduction 機械轉導 同 Cytoskeleton 細胞骨架大圖景 (Big Picture)Mechanotransduction 機械轉導將 physical forces 物理力轉化為 biochemical responses 生化反應，透過 cytoskeleton 細胞骨架實現，對骨同血管健康至關重要。Key Terms: Shear stress 剪切應力, Tension 張力, Actin filaments (Microfilaments) 肌動蛋白絲 (微絲), Microtubules (MTs) 微管, Intermediate filaments (IFs) 中間絲, Treadmilling 踏車運動, Dynamic instability 動態不穩定性, Stress shielding 應力遮擋。 Active Recall Bank 主動回憶庫 What sequence in fibronectin binds integrins? (RGD) Name two types of fibronectin. (Soluble plasma, insoluble cellular) Focal adhesions in 2D are ____; in 3D ____. (punctate, fibrillar) Chemotaxis responds to ____ gradients. (chemical) Ligand range: distant via blood? (Endocrine) Central paradigm steps: Initiation, ____, Gene Activation. (Transduction) GPCRs have ____ transmembrane segments. (7) RTKs phosphorylate ____ residues. (tyrosine) Cytoskeleton filaments: Actin, ____, Intermediate. (Microtubules) Treadmilling occurs in ____ filaments. (actin) BMP signaling uses ____ proteins. (Smads) Wnt canonical pathway stabilizes ____. (β-catenin) Stress shielding causes bone ____. (atrophy 萎縮) Myosin uses ____ for force. (ATP) MTOC (Microtubule Organizing Center) 微管組織中心 organizes ____. (microtubules 微管) RGD stands for --____. (Arg-Gly-Asp) Taxis type for light: ____. (Phototaxis 趨光性) Translation starts with codon ____. (AUG) Ion channels can be ____-gated or ____-gated. (voltage 電壓, ligand 配體) Paclitaxel ____ microtubules. (stabilizes 穩定)
15-Minute Mock Exam 15分鐘模擬考試 Explain the role of RGD in cell adhesion and its TE applications.
RGD (Arg-Gly-Asp) is the integrin-binding site in fibronectin promoting attachment; in TE, short RGD peptides functionalize scaffolds for cell adhesion without full proteins.
Compare focal adhesions in 2D vs 3D environments, with NPC example.
2D: punctate, ligand-sensitive; 3D: fibrillar, remodeling-focused; for NPCs, 3D stemness maintenance is insensitive to ligands, prioritizing remodeling.
Describe chemotaxis and its measurement.
Directed migration to chemical gradients; assessed by polarized morphology and proportion of cells moving toward/away from attractant, depending on concentration and gradient steepness.
Outline the central signal transduction paradigm.
Initiation: ligand-receptor binding; Transduction: cascade activates nuclear TF; Gene activation: TF binds DNA, leading to transcription, translation, and physiological changes.
How does Wnt canonical pathway work?
Ligand binds Fz/LRP, stabilizes β-catenin, which enters nucleus and interacts with TCF/Lef for gene transcription in development and cancer.
One-Page Cheat Sheet Summary 一頁速查表總結
Section 部分 Key Points 要點 (中英對照)
ECM/Adhesion 細胞外基質/黏附 Fibronectin 纖連蛋白: RGD binds α5β1/αVβ3; Focal adhesions 黏著斑: 2D punctate vs 3D fibrillar; NPCs 神經祖細胞: remodeling 重塑 > binding 結合 in 3D.
Migration 遷移 Cycle 循環: Attach-Contract-Detach 黏附-收縮-脫離; Taxis 趨向性: Chemo 趨化性 (gradients 梯度), Hapto 趨觸性 (adhesives 黏附分子); Defects 缺陷: embryonic issues 胚胎問題.
Signaling 信號傳導 Ligands 配體: Endocrine內分泌/Paracrine旁分泌/Autocrine自分泌/Juxtacrine近分泌; GFs 生長因子: VEGF/TGF-β; Paradigm 法則: Bind結合 → Cascade級聯 → Gene基因.
Receptors 受體 GPCR: 7TM, dissociate G解離G蛋白; RTK: Dimer二聚/P-Tyr酪氨酸磷酸化/Ras-ERK通路; TGF-β: Ser/Thr絲氨酸/蘇氨酸/Smads; Wnt: β-catenin/TCF.
Transport 運輸 Hydrophobic 疏水性: Diffuse擴散 (NO/AA); Ion Channels 離子通道: Voltage/Ligand gated電壓/配體門控 (Ca2+/Na+); Optogenetics 光遺傳學: Light control光控制.
Cytoskeleton 細胞骨架 Actin 肌動蛋白: Polymer聚合/Treadmill踏車運動/Myosin肌球蛋白; MT微管: Dynamic動態/GTP/Paclitaxel紫杉醇; Mechanotransduction機械轉導: Forces力 → Signals信號 (Wolff’s Law沃爾夫定律).
TE Apps 組織工程應用 Dermal matrix 真皮基質: Cell invasion細胞侵入; Growth factors 生長因子 in scaffolds支架; Avoid stress shielding 避免應力遮擋 in implants植入物.
English Version BelowTopic 1 – Fibronectin and Integrin BindingBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Fibronectin is a key ECM glycoprotein facilitating cell adhesion via integrins, crucial for tissue structure and signaling.Mind map: Fibronectin → Types (Soluble Plasma → Bloodstream → Hepatocytes; Insoluble Cellular → Secreted by Fibroblasts) → Binds to (Integrins → RGD Sequence → α5β1/αVβ3; ECM Components → Collagen/Fibrin/Heparan Sulfate) → Enables Cell Attachment. Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) 🔴 Fibronectin is the major ECM glycoprotein binding integrins. 🟢 Binds other ECM like collagen, fibrin, heparan sulfate proteoglycans. 🔴 Two types: soluble plasma (hepatocytes) and insoluble cellular (fibroblasts). 🟢 Integrin binding uses RGD sequence (Arg-Gly-Asp). 🔴 Key integrins: α5β1 and αVβ3 on cell surface. 🟢 Short RGD peptides can mimic fibronectin for cell attachment. 🔵 RGD enables focal adhesion formation. 🟢 Plasma fibronectin circulates in blood; cellular is matrix-integrated. 🔴 RGD is the primary cell attachment site. 🟢 Binding promotes clustering into focal adhesions. 🔵 Used in biomaterials for cell adhesion engineering. 🟢 Insoluble form secreted by many cells like fibroblasts. 🔴 Heparan sulfate binding aids in growth factor localization. 🟢 Fibronectin domains bind TGF-β and bFGF actively.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Fibronectin Structure:NH2 - [Type I Repeats] - [Type II] - [Type III] - COOHBindings: Fibrin/Heparan (I), Collagen (I/II), Integrin (III - RGD), Heparan/Fibrin (III) RGD Binding:Cell Surface Integrin (α5β1/αVβ3) ⇄ RGD Sequence → Cell Attachment
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Aspect Soluble Plasma Fibronectin (X) Insoluble Cellular Fibronectin (Y)
Source Produced by hepatocytes Secreted by fibroblasts etc.
Location Circulates in bloodstream Integrated into ECM
Function Coagulation, wound healing Cell adhesion, matrix structure
Solubility Soluble Insoluble
Production Liver-specific Various cell types
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Mistaking RGD as the only binding site (trap: forget synergy regions like PHSRN). Confusing fibronectin types: assuming all fibronectin is insoluble in ECM. Overlooking RGD’s role in synthetic peptides for TE applications.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks RGD: “Really Good Dance” – Arg-Gly-Asp makes cells “dance” (attach and move). Fibronectin types: “Plasma Soluble in Blood, Cellular Insoluble in Matrix” – PSB-CIM.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)What is the role of the RGD sequence in fibronectin?
The RGD (Arg-Gly-Asp) sequence is the primary integrin-binding site facilitating cell attachment via α5β1 and αVβ3 integrins.
Compare soluble and insoluble fibronectin.
Soluble plasma fibronectin circulates in blood from hepatocytes for systemic roles; insoluble cellular fibronectin is secreted locally for ECM integration and adhesion.
Topic 2 – Focal AdhesionsBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Focal adhesions are integrin-mediated clusters linking ECM to cytoskeleton, transmitting signals and forces.Mind map: Focal Adhesion → Integrin Binds (Collagen/Fibronectin → RGD) → Cluster Formation → Components (Vinculin/Talin/FAK/Actin/Myosin) → Signals to Nucleus → 2D (Punctate) vs 3D (Diffuse). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) 🔴 Integrins bind ECM like collagen/fibronectin to form focal adhesions. 🟢 Focal adhesions are cell adhesion clusters. 🔴 Green fluorescence often labels focal adhesions in imaging. 🟢 2D adhesions are punctate; 3D are fibrillar/diffuse. 🔵 Transmit mechanical signals to nucleus via cytoskeleton. 🟢 Involve proteins: vinculin, talin, paxillin, FAK. 🔴 Neural progenitor cells: remodeling > binding in 3D. 🟢 NPC stemness insensitive to adhesive ligands in 3D. 🔵 Used in TE for scaffold design mimicking ECM. 🟢 Forces from actin-myosin contraction. 🔴 Turnover regulates migration. 🟢 FAK phosphorylates for signaling. 🔵 3D environments alter adhesion morphology.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Focal Adhesion Assembly:ECM (Fibronectin) → Integrin → Talin/Vinculin → Actin/Myosin → Force Transmission → Nucleus (Gene Expression) 2D vs 3D:2D: Flat substrate → Discrete spots3D: Matrix embedding → Elongated fibrils

Imaging Bright spots Linear extensions
NPC Response Ligand-sensitive Remodeling-focused
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Assuming 2D and 3D adhesions are identical (trap: ignore dimensionality in TE designs). Forgetting focal adhesions’ role in signaling, not just adhesion. Overlooking NPC insensitivity to ligands in 3D.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks Focal Adhesion: “FACTS” – Fibronectin-Actin Connection Transmits Signals. 2D vs 3D: “Punctate in Plane (2D), Fibrils in Full (3D)”.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Describe focal adhesion formation.
Integrins bind ECM (e.g., fibronectin via RGD), clustering with talin/vinculin/actin to form adhesions transmitting signals.
How do focal adhesions differ in 2D vs 3D?
In 2D, they are punctate for strong anchorage; in 3D, fibrillar for remodeling, as seen in NPC maintenance.
Topic 3 – Cell Migration and ChemotaxisBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Cell migration involves cytoskeleton reorganization for movement, guided by taxis like chemotaxis in development and repair.Mind map: Migration → Mechanisms (Attachment → Contraction → Detachment) → Cytoskeleton (Actin Polymerization → Myosin Force) → Taxis (Chemo → Gradient; Hapto → Adhesive; Photo → Light) → Applications (Embryogenesis → Wound Healing). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) 🔴 Cells migrate via attachment, contraction, detachment. 🟢 Not just elastic bags; involve cytoskeleton reorganization. 🔴 Chemotaxis: movement by chemical gradients (e.g., cAMP). 🟢 Polarized morphology in migrating cells. 🔵 Strength depends on concentration and gradient steepness. 🟢 Taxis types: topo (turning to stimulus), klino (less turning oriented), ortho (speed increase oriented). 🔴 Defects cause embryonic malformations, epilepsy. 🟢 In skin repair: cells invade dermal matrix. 🔵 Actin polymerization initiates; myosin maintains. 🟢 Focal adhesions as traction points. 🔴 Neutrophil chases bacterium via chemotaxis. 🟢 Cilia motion for fluid movement. 🔵 Embryonic: blastocyst implantation requires migration.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Migration Cycle:1. Protrusion (Actin Polymerization at Leading Edge)2. Adhesion (Focal Contacts Form)3. Contraction (Myosin Pulls Body Forward)4. Release (Rear Detachment) Chemotaxis:Cell → Detect Gradient (High [Chemoattractant] → Movement) → Polarization
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Aspect Chemotaxis (X) Haptotaxis (Y)
Stimulus Dissolved chemicals Insoluble adhesives
Mechanism Gradient sensing Adhesive gradient
Example Neutrophil to bacteria Cell on ECM gradient
Response Directed speed/turning Enhanced adhesion-move
TE Application Wound healing signals Scaffold design
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Confusing chemotaxis with random migration (trap: ignore gradient dependency). Forgetting migration’s role in embryogenesis defects. Overlooking contraction’s myosin dependence.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks Taxis Types: “TACO” – Topo/ortho (Turn/Accelerate), Chemo/Hapto (Chemical/Adhesive Orientation). Migration: “ACD” – Attach-Contract-Detach.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Explain chemotaxis.
Directed migration responding to chemical gradients, with polarized cells moving toward higher concentrations.
Why is cell migration crucial in embryonic development?
Defects lead to implantation failure, malformed tissues, or congenital abnormalities like epilepsy.
Topic 4 – Signaling Ligands and TypesBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Ligands initiate signaling for coordination in tissue development, classified by type, range, and function.Mind map: Ligands → Molecular (Diffusible → Growth Factors/Steroids; ECM → Proteoglycans/Collagen) → Range (Endocrine → Distant; Paracrine → Nearby; Autocrine → Self; Juxtacrine → Contact) → Function (Growth Factors → VEGF/TGF-β; Hormones/Cytokines/Chemokines). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) 🔴 Ligands: diffusible (soluble proteins, steroids) or ECM-bound. 🟢 Range: endocrine (distant, blood), paracrine (local), autocrine (self), juxtacrine (contact). 🔴 Growth factors stimulate proliferation/differentiation (VEGF, TGF-β, bFGF, EGF). 🟢 Hormones: messengers via circulation. 🔵 Cytokines affect other cells; chemokines are chemotactic. 🟢 Matrikines: ECM fragments with activity. 🔴 Thrombospondins inhibit angiogenesis. 🟢 Signaling: ligand generation → response (proliferation, migration, ECM production). 🔵 TE applications: growth factors in scaffolds. 🟢 Parathyroid hormone: endocrine for bone remodeling. 🔴 Interleukins: paracrine in skin. 🟢 Autocrine: self-response. 🔵 Juxtacrine requires cell-cell contact.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Ligand Classification:Type: Diffusible vs ECMRange: Endocrine (Blood) → Paracrine (Local) → Autocrine (Self) → Juxtacrine (Contact)Function: GF (Prolif/Diff) → Hormone (Systemic) → Cytokine (Cell Behavior) Signaling Initiation:Ligand Bind Receptor → Transduction → Gene Activation
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Aspect Endocrine Ligands (X) Paracrine Ligands (Y)
Range Distant targets Nearby cells
Transport Blood/extracellular Diffusion
Example Parathyroid hormone Interleukins
Speed Slower (systemic) Faster (local)
TE Use Systemic delivery Local scaffold release
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Mixing range with function (trap: call growth factors hormones). Forgetting juxtacrine needs physical contact. Assuming all ligands are soluble.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks Ranges: “EPAJ” – Endocrine-Paracrine-Autocrine-Juxtacrine (Every Person Acts Justly). Growth Factors: “Very Tall Big Elephants” – VEGF/TGF-β/bFGF/EGF.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Classify ligands by range.
Endocrine: distant via blood; paracrine: local; autocrine: self; juxtacrine: contact-based.
Name key growth factors in TE.
VEGF (angiogenesis), TGF-β (differentiation), bFGF (proliferation), EGF (epithelial growth).
Topic 5 – Central Paradigm of Signal TransductionBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Signal transduction converts extracellular ligands into intracellular responses via receptors and cascades leading to gene activation.Mind map: Paradigm → Initiation (Ligand Bind Receptor → Activation) → Transduction (Cascade → TF Activation) → Gene (TF Binds → Transcription → Protein Synthesis). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) 🔴 Initiation: ligand binds membrane receptor, changes activity. 🟢 Transduction: activates cascade to nuclear TF. 🔴 Gene activation: TF binds regulatory sequences → mRNA/protein. 🟢 TF: DNA-binding proteins; may translocate or activate. 🔵 Involves second messengers (cAMP, Ca2+, NO). 🟢 Transcription: RNA polymerase uses rNTPs. 🔴 Translation: mRNA → protein via ribosomes, start AUG. 🟢 Protein folding: ER → Golgi → secretion. 🔵 mRNA vaccines: modified bases reduce inflammation. 🟢 Protein structure: primary (AA sequence) to quaternary. 🔴 Central dogma: signal → transduction → gene expression. 🟢 Relocalization: e.g., NFκB from cytosol to nucleus. 🔵 Phosphorylation common in cascades.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Central Paradigm:1. Ligand + Receptor → Activated Receptor2. Cascade (Phosphorylation/Second Messengers) → Active TF3. TF + DNA → Transcription → mRNA → Translation → Protein Transcription:DNA → RNA Polymerase → mRNA (rNTPs: A/U/G/C)
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Aspect Transcription (X) Translation (Y)
Location Nucleus Cytoplasm/Ribosomes
Input DNA mRNA
Output mRNA Protein
Building Blocks rNTPs Amino Acids
Initiation Promoter/TF Start Codon AUG
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Confusing transcription with translation (trap: mix RNA vs protein synthesis). Forgetting second messengers in transduction. Overlooking protein processing post-translation.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks Paradigm: “I-T-G” – Initiation-Transduction-Gene Activation. Codons: “AUG Starts Translation” – Always Use Gloves (safety hook).
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Outline the central paradigm.
Ligand binds receptor (initiation), triggers cascade (transduction), activates TF for gene expression.
What is gene activation?
TF binds DNA regulatory sequences, increasing transcription rate to produce mRNA and proteins.
Topic 6 – G-Protein Coupled ReceptorsBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)GPCRs transmit diverse signals via G-protein dissociation, involved in senses and regulation.Mind map: GPCR → 7 Transmembrane → Ligand Bind → G-Protein (α/β/γ Dissociate) → Effectors (Adenylate Cyclase/PLC) → Second Messengers (cAMP/IP3/Ca2+). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) 🔴 GPCRs: 7 transmembrane segments. 🟢 Respond to photons, amines, hormones, proteins. 🔴 Agonist binding dissociates G-protein subunits. 🟢 α subunit or β/γ interact with effectors. 🔵 Involved in vision (photoisomerization), smell, immunity. 🟢 Autonomic NS: blood pressure, heart rate. 🔴 Visual: rhodopsin activation. 🟢 Smell: olfactory receptors. 🔵 Immune: chemokine receptors. 🟢 Dissociation: GDP → GTP exchange.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)GPCR Signaling:Ligand + GPCR → G-Protein Activation (GDP → GTP) → α Dissociates → Effector (e.g., AC → cAMP) Topology:Extracellular Loops → Ligand Bind → 7 TM Helices → Intracellular G-Protein Interaction
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Aspect Before Binding (X) After Binding (Y)
G-Protein State Bound (αβγ) Dissociated (α + βγ)
Nucleotide GDP GTP
Effect Inactive Effector Activation
Example Resting Receptor Signal Transduction
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Forgetting 7 TM structure (trap: confuse with RTKs). Assuming all ligands bind extracellularly. Overlooking β/γ subunit roles.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks GPCR: “7 Seas” – 7 Transmembrane for Signaling Seas (diverse ligands). Dissociation: “Agonist Kicks GDP Out for GTP Party”.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Describe GPCR activation.
Ligand binds, causing G-protein interaction and dissociation of α (GTP-bound) from βγ.
GPCRs’ roles?
Vision (photons), smell (odorants), immunity (chemokines), autonomic regulation.
Topic 7 – Receptor Tyrosine Kinases and PathwaysBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)RTKs dimerize upon ligand binding, phosphorylating for cascades in growth and angiogenesis.Mind map: RTK → Ligand (e.g., VEGF) → Dimerization → Phosphorylation → Cascade (Ras/Raf/MEK/ERK) → TF (Ets-1) → Gene Expression. Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) 🔴 RTKs: dimerize, autophosphorylate tyrosines. 🟢 Include VEGF-R, PDGF-R for vessel assembly. 🔴 VEGF: endothelial differentiation. 🟢 PDGF-B: recruits mural cells. 🔵 Cascade: Sos → Ras → Raf → MEK → ERK → Nucleus. 🟢 TGF-β: serine/threonine kinases, Smad pathway. 🔴 BMPs: bone induction via Smads. 🟢 Wnt: canonical (β-catenin → TCF/Lef), noncanonical (polarity/Ca2+). 🔵 Involved in cancer, development. 🟢 LRP5/6 co-receptors in Wnt. 🔴 Fz: GPCR-like in Wnt.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)RTK Pathway:Ligand → Dimer → P-Tyr → Adapter → Ras-GTP → Raf → MEK → ERK → TF BMP Signaling:BMP + Type II R → Type I P → Smad1/5/8 P → Smad4 Complex → Nucleus
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Aspect RTK (X) Ser/Thr Kinase (Y)
Phosphorylation Tyrosine Serine/Threonine
Example VEGF-R TGF-β/BMP R
Downstream Ras/MAPK Smads
Function Growth/Angiogenesis Differentiation/Bone
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Confusing Tyr vs Ser/Thr kinases (trap: mix VEGF with BMP). Forgetting dimerization in RTKs. Overlooking Wnt’s multiple pathways.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks RTK: “Dimer-Phos-Cascade” – Dinner Party Cascade (dimerize to start). Wnt: “Beta Cat in Canon” – β-Catenin Canonical.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Outline VEGF signaling.
VEGF binds VEGF-R2, activates Ras via Sos, leading to ERK phosphorylation and Ets-1 activation for angiogenic genes.
Describe BMP pathway.
BMP binds receptors, phosphorylates Smad1/5/8, complexes with Smad4, translocates to nucleus for transcription.
Topic 8 – Mechanotransduction and CytoskeletonBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Mechanotransduction converts physical forces into biochemical responses via cytoskeleton, vital for bone and vessel health.Mind map: Forces (Shear/Tension) → Sense (Integrins/Junctions) → Transmit (Cytoskeleton → Actin/MT/IF) → Response (Gene Expression) → Applications (Stress Shielding/Osteoporosis). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) 🔴 Mechanotransduction: physical stimulus → physiological change. 🟢 Cytoskeleton: actin (microfilaments), microtubules, intermediate filaments. 🔴 Actin: G-actin → F-actin polymerization; (+) end growth. 🟢 Myosin: ATP → force for contraction. 🔵 Treadmilling: add at (+), remove at (-). 🟢 Microtubules: α/β tubulin; dynamic instability. 🔴 MTOC organizes polarity. 🟢 Stress shielding: prosthesis reduces bone load → atrophy. 🔵 Endothelial: shear → alignment via PECAM-1/VE-cad/VEGFR2. 🟢 Fibroblasts contract collagen matrix. 🔴 Wolff’s Law: stress influences bone strength. 🟢 Optogenetics: light-sensitive channels control activity. 🔵 Paclitaxel stabilizes MT, inhibits division.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Actin Dynamics:G-Actin (ATP) → Nucleation → Elongation (+) End → Treadmilling (Hydrolysis ADP) Cytoskeleton:Actin → Contraction/MigrationMT → Transport/DivisionIF → Stability
Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Aspect Actin Filaments (X) Microtubules (Y)
Monomer G-Actin α/β Tubulin
Function Motility/Contraction Transport/Division
Nucleotide ATP GTP
Dynamics Treadmilling Dynamic Instability
Motor Protein Myosin Kinesin/Dynein
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Treating cells as simple viscous bags (trap: ignore cytoskeleton). Forgetting GTP/ATP in dynamics. Overlooking stress shielding in implants.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks Cytoskeleton: “AMI” – Actin (Movement), MT (Transport), IF (Integrity). Actin Ends: “+ Grows, - Goes” (add +, remove -).
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)What is mechanotransduction?
Conversion of physical forces (e.g., shear) into biochemical responses like gene expression via cytoskeleton.
Describe actin polymerization.
G-actin nucleates with ATP, elongates at (+) end, hydrolyzes to ADP, treadmills with disassembly at (-).
Active Recall Bank What sequence in fibronectin binds integrins? (RGD) Name two types of fibronectin. (Soluble plasma, insoluble cellular) Focal adhesions in 2D are ____; in 3D ____. (punctate, fibrillar) Chemotaxis responds to ____ gradients. (chemical) Ligand range: distant via blood? (Endocrine) Central paradigm steps: Initiation, ____, Gene Activation. (Transduction) GPCRs have ____ transmembrane segments. (7) RTKs phosphorylate ____ residues. (tyrosine) Cytoskeleton filaments: Actin, ____, Intermediate. (Microtubules) Treadmilling occurs in ____ filaments. (actin) BMP signaling uses ____ proteins. (Smads) Wnt canonical pathway stabilizes ____. (β-catenin) Stress shielding causes bone ____. (atrophy) Myosin uses ____ for force. (ATP) MTOC organizes ____. (microtubules) RGD stands for --____. (Arg-Gly-Asp) Taxis type for light: ____. (Phototaxis) Translation starts with codon ____. (AUG) Ion channels can be ____-gated or ____-gated. (voltage, ligand) Paclitaxel ____ microtubules. (stabilizes)
15-Minute Mock Exam Explain the role of RGD in cell adhesion and its TE applications.
RGD (Arg-Gly-Asp) is the integrin-binding site in fibronectin promoting attachment; in TE, short RGD peptides functionalize scaffolds for cell adhesion without full proteins.
Compare focal adhesions in 2D vs 3D environments, with NPC example.
2D: punctate, ligand-sensitive; 3D: fibrillar, remodeling-focused; for NPCs, 3D stemness maintenance is insensitive to ligands, prioritizing remodeling.
Describe chemotaxis and its measurement.
Directed migration to chemical gradients; assessed by polarized morphology and proportion of cells moving toward/away from attractant, depending on concentration and gradient steepness.
Outline the central signal transduction paradigm.
Initiation: ligand-receptor binding; Transduction: cascade activates nuclear TF; Gene activation: TF binds DNA, leading to transcription, translation, and physiological changes.
How does Wnt canonical pathway work?
Ligand binds Fz/LRP, stabilizes β-catenin, which enters nucleus and interacts with TCF/Lef for gene transcription in development and cancer.
One-Page Cheat Sheet Summary
Section Key Points
ECM/Adhesion Fibronectin: RGD binds α5β1/αVβ3; Focal: 2D punctate vs 3D fibrillar; NPCs: remodeling > binding.
Migration Cycle: Attach-Contract-Detach; Taxis: Chemo (gradients), Hapto (adhesives); Defects: embryonic issues.
Signaling Ligands: Endocrine/Paracrine/Autocrine/Juxtacrine; GFs: VEGF/TGF-β; Paradigm: Bind → Cascade → Gene.
Receptors GPCR: 7TM, dissociate G; RTK: Dimer/P-Tyr/Ras-ERK; TGF-β: Ser/Thr/Smads; Wnt: β-catenin/TCF.
Transport Hydrophobic: Diffuse (NO/AA); Ion Channels: Voltage/Ligand (Ca2+/Na+); Optogenetics: Light control.
Cytoskeleton Actin: Polymer/Treadmill/Myosin; MT: Dynamic/GTP/Paclitaxel; Mechano: Forces → Signals (Wolff’s Law).
TE Apps Dermal matrix: Cell invasion; Growth factors in scaffolds; Avoid stress shielding in implants.

entry.title: BME 2104 Tissue Engineering – 5
entry.content:
BME 2104 Tissue Engineering – 5Lecture Overview 每年必出嘅八大題目 (Top 8 topics that appear every year): Tensegrity Model 張拉整體模型 🔴 Mechanoreceptors 機械感受器 and Force Propagation 力傳播 🟢 Integrins 整合素 and Focal Adhesions 黏著斑 🔴 Integrin-Dependent Mechanosignaling 依賴整合素的機械信號轉導 🟢 Effects of Cell Shape 細胞形狀 and Mechanical Forces 機械力 on Differentiation 分化 🔵 Scaffold Requirements and Properties 支架要求與特性 🟢 Types of Scaffolds 支架類型 (Bioinert 生物惰性, Bioactive 生物活性, Bioresorbable 生物可吸收) 🔴 Scaffold Materials 支架材料 (Natural vs Synthetic 天然 vs 合成, Metals 金屬, Ceramics 陶瓷) 🔵
顏色標記 (Colour code): 🔴 Memorise 必須背誦 | 🟢 Understand 必須理解 | 🔵 Apply 必須應用
Topic 1 – Tissue Engineering Paradigm 組織工程範式大圖景 (Big Picture)The tissue engineering paradigm integrates cells 細胞, biocompatible materials 生物相容性材料, biochemical factors 生化因子 like growth factors 生長因子, and biophysical factors 生物物理因子 such as cyclic mechanical loading 週期性機械負荷 to regenerate tissues. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Paradigm 範式 → Cells 細胞 (source 來源) → Materials 材料 (scaffolds 支架) → Biochemical 生化因子 (growth factors 生長因子) → Biophysical 生物物理因子 (mechanical loading 機械負荷) → Outcomes 結果 (tissue regeneration 組織再生) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Core components 核心組成部分: cells 細胞, biocompatible materials 生物相容性材料, biochemical factors 生化因子 (e.g., growth factors), biophysical factors 生物物理因子 (e.g., cyclic mechanical loading). Aims to regenerate 3D tissues using a multidisciplinary approach 多學科方法. Cells 細胞 provide the building blocks for new tissue formation. Materials 材料 act as scaffolds 支架 for cell attachment 細胞黏附 and growth 生長. Biochemical factors 生化因子 stimulate cell proliferation 細胞增殖 and differentiation 分化. Biophysical factors 生物物理因子 mimic in vivo 體內 mechanical environments to guide tissue development. Paradigm emphasizes biomimicry 仿生 of natural ECM 細胞外基質. Applications include hard tissues 硬組織 like bone and soft tissues 軟組織 like vascular. Challenges: controlling differentiation, proliferation, and immune response 免疫反應. Mechanical environment 機械環境 plays a key role in cell signaling 細胞信號傳導 and phenotype 表型. Scaffolds must support nutrient diffusion 營養擴散 and vascularization 血管化. Cyclic stretching 週期性拉伸 is essential for constructs like myocardium 心肌. Integration of factors ensures synchronized tissue function 同步的組織功能.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Tissue Engineering Triad 組織工程三要素:Cells 細胞 + Scaffolds (Materials) 支架 (材料) + Signals (Biochemical + Biophysical) 信號 (生化+生物物理) → Regenerated Tissue 再生組織
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Biochemical vs Biophysical Factors 生化 vs 生物物理因子
Aspect 方面 Biochemical Factors 生化因子 Biophysical Factors 生物物理因子
Definition 定義 Chemical signals like growth factors 化學信號，如生長因子 Physical stimuli like mechanical loading 物理刺激，如機械負荷
Role 作用 Promote proliferation/differentiation 促進增殖/分化 Influence shape, viability, and signaling 影響形狀、活力和信號傳導
Examples 例子 VEGF, BMP Shear stress 剪切應力, cyclic stretch 週期性拉伸
Mechanism 機制 Bind receptors, activate pathways 結合受體，激活通路 Transmit via cytoskeleton to nucleus 通過細胞骨架傳遞至細胞核
Exam Focus 考試重點 Pathway details 通路細節 Tensegrity integration 張拉整體整合
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 混淆生物物理因子同生化因子 (Mistaking biophysical for biochemical): 考官可能會要求區分兩者效果；唔好混淆機械負荷同生長因子。 忽略整合性 (Overlooking integration): 陷阱題目會問範式而不提及所有四個組成部分。 假設所有因子作用相同 (Assuming all factors are equal): 答案中經常會忽視生物物理因子嘅重要性。
Topic 2 – Tensegrity Model 張拉整體模型大圖景 (Big Picture)The tensegrity model 張拉整體模型 describes cells as prestressed structures 預應力結構 where tension 張力 and compression 壓力 elements balance forces, enabling rapid mechanical signal transmission for mechanotransduction 機械轉導. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Tensegrity 張拉整體 → Architectural 結構性 (tension-compression synergy 張力-壓力協同) → Cellular 細胞性 (actin tension 肌動蛋白張力, microtubules compression 微管壓力) → Pre-stress 預應力 (contractile tension 收縮張力) → Force Propagation 力傳播 (rapid, long-distance 快速，長距離) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Championed by Don Ingber as alternative to elastic bag model 彈性袋模型. Cells and nuclei are “hard-wired 硬連線“ to respond to physical stimuli via integrins 整合素. Tension elements 張力元素: actin filaments 肌動蛋白絲; compression 壓力元素: microtubules 微管. Pre-stress 預應力 from actomyosin contraction 肌動球蛋白收縮 balances forces. Stabilizes cell structure and morphology 形態. Forces transmitted at ECM/cell contacts ECM/細胞接觸點, not equally over surface. Inhibited by integrin antagonists 整合素拮抗劑 (shear stress, strain effects). Provides basis for mechanotransduction 機械轉導 linking physical to molecular events. Explains transmission to nucleus. Synergy between balanced tension and compression. Architectural system 結構系統 stabilizes by counteracting forces. Applies to natural and artificial forms (Buckminster Fuller). Prestressed structures 預應力結構 promote long-distance force propagation 長距離力傳播. Faster than chemical signals (1000x via cytoskeleton). Contrasts with homogeneous material models 均質材料模型 where forces decay rapidly.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Tensegrity Balance 張拉整體平衡:Tension 張力 (Actin) + Compression 壓力 (Microtubules) + Pre-stress 預應力 = Stable Network 穩定網絡
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Tensegrity vs Elastic Bag Model 張拉整體 vs 彈性袋模型
Aspect 方面 Tensegrity Model 張拉整體模型 Elastic Bag Model 彈性袋模型
Structure 結構 Prestressed tension-compression 預應力張力-壓力 Viscous fluid in bag 袋中黏性流體
Force Transmission 力傳遞 Rapid along cytoskeleton 沿細胞骨架快速傳遞 Slow diffusion 緩慢擴散
Response 響應 Immediate to physical stimuli 對物理刺激即時響應 Delayed chemical 化學延遲
Key Elements 關鍵元素 Actin, microtubules, integrins 肌動蛋白，微管，整合素 Membrane, cytosol 膜，細胞質
Applications 應用 Mechanotransduction explanation 解釋機械轉導 Basic cell mechanics 基礎細胞力學
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 忘記預應力 (Forgetting pre-stress): 陷阱題在解釋力平衡時不提肌動球蛋白。 混淆元素 (Confusing elements): 混淆張力（肌動蛋白）和壓力（微管）。 低估速度 (Underestimating speed): 考官測試點解機械信號傳遞快過化學信號。
Topic 3 – Mechanoreceptors 機械感受器大圖景 (Big Picture)Mechanoreceptors 機械感受器 are plasma membrane proteins 質膜蛋白 that sense external mechanical signals and relay them intracellularly, including integrins 整合素, cadherins 鈣黏蛋白, and stretch-activated ion channels 牽張激活離子通道. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Mechanoreceptors 機械感受器 → Integrins 整合素 (ECM-cytoskeleton link ECM-細胞骨架連結) → Cadherins 鈣黏蛋白 (cell-cell adhesion 細胞間黏附) → Ion Channels 離子通道 (ion flux on stretch 拉伸時離子流) → Outcomes 結果 (signaling 信號傳導, gene expression 基因表達) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Located in plasma membrane 質膜 to sense and relay signals. Integrins 整合素: Link ECM to cytoskeleton via focal adhesions 黏著斑, alter cytoskeleton directly. Cadherins 鈣黏蛋白: Form adherens junctions 黏附連接 for cell-cell force transmission. Stretch-activated ion channels 牽張激活離子通道: Selective for Na+, K+, Ca2+, Cl-; involved in signaling and gene expression. Activated by membrane deformation 膜變形; may activate or inactivate on stretch. Produce intracellular consequences like Ca2+ flux 鈣離子流. Regulate actin polymerization 肌動蛋白聚合 and contact stability 接觸穩定性. Involved in cell signaling, transcription 轉錄, translation 翻譯, protein synthesis 蛋白質合成. Electrochemical gradient 電化學梯度 drives ion movement. Conformational changes 構象變化 with deformation. High ion selectivity 離子選擇性. Essential for mechanotransduction 機械轉導. Cadherins may regulate proliferative signaling 增殖信號 via β-catenin β-連環蛋白. Integrins transmit shear stress 剪切應力 signals.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Ion Channel Activation 離子通道激活:Stretch 拉伸 → Conformational Change 構象變化 → Ion Flux 離子流 (e.g., Ca2+ in) → Signaling Cascade 信號級聯
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Integrins vs Cadherins 整合素 vs 鈣黏蛋白
Aspect 方面 Integrins 整合素 Cadherins 鈣黏蛋白
Function 功能 ECM-cytoskeleton link ECM-細胞骨架連結 Cell-cell adhesion 細胞間黏附
Location 位置 Focal adhesions 黏著斑 Adherens junctions 黏附連接
Response 響應 Alter cytoskeleton 改變細胞骨架 Regulate force transmission 調節力傳遞
Signaling 信號傳導 FAK, ERK activation FAK, ERK激活 β-catenin to nucleus β-連環蛋白入核
Examples 例子 Shear stress induction 誘導剪切應力響應 Tension stabilization 張力穩定化
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 混淆整合素同鈣黏蛋白 (Confusing integrins with cadherins): 陷阱題問細胞間 vs 細胞-ECM。 忽略離子選擇性 (Ignoring ion selectivity): 考官會問特定離子（Na+, K+, Ca2+）。 忽略失活 (Overlooking deactivation): 部分通道拉伸時會失活。
Topic 4 – Integrins 整合素 and Focal Adhesions 黏著斑大圖景 (Big Picture)Integrins 整合素 are transmembrane proteins 跨膜蛋白 mediating cell-ECM binding 細胞-ECM結合 and force transmission, forming focal adhesions 黏著斑 that mature under tension 張力 to activate signaling pathways 信號通路. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Integrins 整合素 → Ligand Binding 配體結合 → Focal Complexes 黏著複合體 (precursor 前體) → Focal Adhesions 黏著斑 (mature 成熟) → Signaling 信號 (FAK, ERK) → Cellular Responses 細胞響應 (shape 形狀, motility 運動性) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Integrins elicit signaling similar to shear stress in endothelial cells 內皮細胞. Transmit forces from ECM to cytoskeleton 細胞骨架. Convert physical stimuli to bioelectrical/biochemical signals 生物電/生化信號. Focal complexes 黏著複合體: Small, punctate 點狀, at cell periphery 細胞邊緣; precursors to FAs. Move centripetally 向心地, elongate into FAs. Focal adhesions 黏著斑: Larger, proximal 近端; require tension 張力 for development. Formation: Integrin occupation 整合素佔據, clustering 聚集, tensile force 張力 exertion. Involve proteins like vinculin, talin, paxillin. Shear stress 剪切應力 recruits FAK to leading edge 前沿. Tyrosine phosphorylation 酪氨酸磷酸化 leads to ERK activation. FAK mutant FAK突變體 blocks ERK response to stress. Integrin-ECM dynamics 整合素-ECM動態 altered by shear. Forces travel to FAs via ECM or cytoskeleton. Essential for mechanobiology 機械生物學 responses.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)FA Maturation 黏著斑成熟:FC (Minutes) --> Tension 張力 --> FA (Larger, Proximal 更大，近端)
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Focal Complexes vs Focal Adhesions 黏著複合體 vs 黏著斑
Aspect 方面 Focal Complexes (FCs) 黏著複合體 Focal Adhesions (FAs) 黏著斑
Size/Location 大小/位置 Small, peripheral 細小，周邊 Larger, proximal 更大，近端
Formation Time 形成時間 Minutes after contact 接觸後幾分鐘 Evolves from FCs 從FCs演化而來
Role 作用 Precursor, leading edge 前體，前沿 Mature, tension-dependent 成熟，依賴張力
Proteins 蛋白質 Initial integrins 初始整合素 FAK, vinculin, talin
Movement 運動 Centripetal 向心 Stable anchor 穩定錨定
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 混淆FC同FA (Mixing FC and FA): 陷阱題關於時間線或位置。 忘記張力作用 (Forgetting tension role): 答案缺少”張力”對於成熟嘅描述。 忽略信號傳導 (Ignoring signaling): 考官測試FAK-ERK聯繫。
Topic 5 – Mechanosignaling Pathways 機械信號通路大圖景 (Big Picture)Mechanosignaling pathways 機械信號通路 convert mechanical stimuli at cell surfaces into intracellular responses like gene expression 基因表達, involving integrins 整合素, VEGF, Wnt, and nuclear transmission 核傳遞. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Stimuli 刺激 (Shear/Stretch 剪切/拉伸) → Receptors 受體 (Integrins/Cadherins 整合素/鈣黏蛋白) → Pathways 通路 (FAK/ERK, VEGF, Wnt) → Nucleus 細胞核 (SUN proteins, chromatin 染色質) → Responses 響應 (Differentiation/Proliferation 分化/增殖) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Mechanobiology 機械生物學: Responses to shear/stretch at focal adhesions. Pathways: FAK activation, ERK via tyrosine phosphorylation 酪氨酸磷酸化. Shear causes FAK recruitment and rear disappearance. VEGF: Activates Ras-Raf-MEK-ERK pathway, phosphorylates Ets-1 for angiogenic genes 血管生成基因. Nuclear transmission 核傳遞: SUN proteins link to lamina 核纖層, chromatin 染色質 via MARs (Matrix Attachment Regions) 基質附着區. Nuclear actin/myosin 核肌動蛋白/肌球蛋白 control gene positioning 基因定位 and activation. Adherens junctions 黏附連接: Similar to FAs, dynamic link to actin. Tension 張力 at junctions stabilizes AJs, regulates β-catenin signaling β-連環蛋白信號. Wnt pathway: β-catenin dissociation to nucleus for gene expression. Cadherins require force for separation. Vinculin localizes to AJs, binds cytoskeletal proteins. Mechanical signals to nucleus within milliseconds 毫秒. Involved in proliferation, apoptosis 凋亡, differentiation. Examples: Ca2+ flux 鈣離子流 at junctions strengthens contacts.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)VEGF Pathway:VEGF --> VEGF-R2 --> Sos --> Ras-GTP --> Raf --> MEK --> ERK --> Ets-1 --> Genes (MMP-1, etc.)
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Integrin vs Cadherin Pathways 整合素 vs 鈣黏蛋白通路
Aspect 方面 Integrin Pathways 整合素通路 Cadherin Pathways 鈣黏蛋白通路
Activation 激活 Shear/stretch at FAs FAs處的剪切/拉伸 Tension at AJs AJs處的張力
Key Proteins 關鍵蛋白質 FAK, ERK β-catenin, vinculin
Outcome 結果 ERK activation, gene expression ERK激活，基因表達 Proliferation via Wnt 通過Wnt增殖
Location 位置 Cell-ECM 細胞-ECM Cell-cell 細胞-細胞
Debate 爭議 Clear force transmission 明確的力傳遞 Tension response under debate 張力響應存在爭議
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 通路混淆 (Pathway mix-up): 混淆VEGF和Wnt；考官測試序列。 忘記核連結 (Forgetting nuclear link): 陷阱題在解釋毫秒級響應時忽略。 忽略β-連環蛋白 (Overlooking β-catenin): 答案缺少核轉位。
Topic 6 – Effects of Mechanical Forces on Cells 機械力對細胞嘅影響大圖景 (Big Picture)Mechanical forces 機械力 influence cell shape 細胞形狀, viability 活力, motility 運動性, differentiation 分化, and proliferation 增殖, switching genetic programs and guiding tissue formation. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Forces 力 → Cell Shape 細胞形狀 (spreading/deformation 鋪展/變形) → Viability 活力 (apoptosis/growth 凋亡/生長) → Motility 運動性 (lamellipodia 板狀偽足) → Differentiation 分化 (osteo/myogenic 成骨/成肌) → Proliferation 增殖 (stress accumulation 應力積累) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Cell shape changes from force balance 力平衡; spreading if ECM stiffer than cytoskeleton. Nucleus deforms, switches apoptosis/differentiation programs 凋亡/分化程序. Geometric control 幾何控制: Micropatterns 微圖案 determine apoptosis vs growth via focal adhesions. Motility 運動性: Lamellipodia 板狀偽足 at stress concentrations. Differentiation 分化: Acto-myosin contractility 肌動球蛋白收縮性 promotes osteogenesis 成骨. Myotube 肌管: Striations 橫紋 on muscle-stiffness gels 肌肉硬度凝膠. Stiffness 硬度 directs lineage 譜系: Soft for neuron 神經元, medium muscle 肌肉, hard bone 骨. Cadherins: Force induces Ca2+ flux 鈣離子流, actin polymerization 肌動蛋白聚合. Traction microscopy 牽引顯微鏡: Measures force during differentiation. Proliferation 增殖: High at edges/sharp geometries; Rho inhibition Rho抑制 abolishes. Myocardium construct 心肌構建體: Neonatal cells + collagen, cyclic stretch 週期性拉伸 for synchronization. Morphologically similar to adult tissue after 6 days. Tissue shape determines stress accumulation 應力積累 for growth. Mechanical precedent 機械先導 in differentiation.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Stiffness-Differentiation 硬度-分化:Soft (~0.1-1 kPa) → Neuron 神經元Medium (~10 kPa) → Muscle 肌肉Hard (~30 kPa) → Bone 骨
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Shape-Dependent Viability vs Differentiation 形狀依賴活力 vs 分化
Aspect 方面 Shape-Dependent Viability 形狀依賴活力 Shape-Dependent Differentiation 形狀依賴分化
Control 控制 Micropatterned ECM 微圖案化ECM Geometric features/contractility 幾何特徵/收縮性
Outcome 結果 Apoptosis vs growth 凋亡 vs 生長 Osteogenesis, myogenesis 成骨，成肌
Key Factor 關鍵因子 Focal adhesions 黏著斑 Acto-myosin tension 肌動球蛋白張力
Examples 例子 Small patterns → apoptosis 小圖案→凋亡 High contractility → bone 高收縮性→骨
Stiffness Role 硬度作用 N/A Matches tissue (e.g., muscle gels) 匹配組織（如肌肉凝膠）
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 忽略硬度 (Ignoring stiffness): 陷阱題在解釋分化時不提kPa數值。 泛化增殖 (Generalizing proliferation): 增殖在邊緣高，並非均勻。 忘記先導性 (Forgetting precedence): 機械變化先於分化發生。
Topic 7 – Scaffolds Introduction and Requirements 支架簡介與要求大圖景 (Big Picture)Scaffolds 支架 are artificial ECMs 人工細胞外基質 providing 3D support for cell growth, nutrient diffusion 營養擴散, and tissue regeneration, with requirements for porosity 孔隙率, biocompatibility 生物相容性, and mechanical matching 機械匹配. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Scaffolds 支架 → Functions 功能 (support 支撐, degrade 降解, diffuse 擴散) → Requirements 要求 (3D porous 3D多孔, biocompatible 生物相容性, surface chemistry 表面化學, mechanical 機械性) → Biomimetic 仿生 (mimic ECM 模仿ECM) → Applications 應用 (bone 骨, vascular 血管) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Temporary/permanent artificial ECM 人工ECM for 3D regeneration. ECM 細胞外基質: Macromolecules (proteins/carbs 蛋白質/碳水化合物) as space fillers 空間填充物. 2D/3D structures; promote cell growth, degrade. Allow nutrient/waste diffusion 營養/廢物擴散, cell migration 細胞遷移. Encourage blood vessel formation 血管形成. Biomimetic 仿生: Apply natural methods to engineering. Examples: Trabecular 小梁結構 for bone, triphasic 三相 for bone-ligament. Requirements: 3D porous interconnected network 多孔互連網絡. Biocompatible 生物相容性, bioresorbable 生物可吸收 with controllable degradation. Suitable surface for attachment/proliferation/differentiation 黏附/增殖/分化. Mechanical properties 機械特性 match implantation site 植入部位. High porosity 孔隙率 for cell colonization 細胞定植. Tunable biodegradability 生物降解性. Biocompatibility 生物相容性 prevents immune reaction 免疫反應.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Scaffold Requirements 支架要求:Porosity 孔隙率 > 90% + Interconnectivity 互連性 + Degradation Rate 降解速率 = Tissue Growth Match 組織生長匹配
對比表 (Comparison Table) - 2D vs 3D Scaffolds
Aspect 方面 2D Scaffolds 2D支架 3D Scaffolds 3D支架
Structure 結構 Substrate-like 基底樣 Volumetric 體積性
Function 功能 Basic support 基礎支撐 Full tissue regeneration 完全組織再生
Diffusion 擴散 Limited 有限 Enhanced via pores 通過孔隙增強
Applications 應用 In vitro studies 體外研究 In vivo implants 體內植入物
Challenges 挑戰 No depth 無深度 Vascularization 血管化
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 忘記互連性 (Forgetting interconnectivity): 僅有孔隙率不足；陷阱題關於擴散。 機械特性不匹配 (Mismatching mechanics): 答案忽略部位特異性特性。 忽略降解 (Overlooking degradation): 必須與組織形成同步。
Topic 8 – Scaffold Properties and Structures 支架特性與結構大圖景 (Big Picture)Scaffold properties 支架特性 include composition 組成 (polymers/ceramics/metals), macrostructure 宏觀結構, porosity/interconnectivity 孔隙率/互連性, and other factors like surface/volume ratio 表面積/體積比 and degradation 降解 for optimal tissue integration. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Properties 特性 → Composition 組成 (natural/synthetic 天然/合成) → Structure 結構 (macro 宏觀, pores/tubes 孔/管) → Porosity 孔隙率 (200-500μm) → Others 其他 (ratio 比率, strength 強度, non-toxic degradation 無毒降解) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Composed of polymers 聚合物, ceramics 陶瓷, metals 金屬, composites 複合材料. Match tissue: Polymers for soft, metals/ceramics for hard. Porous 多孔 for cell entry, large surface area 表面積, interconnected 互連. Composition: Pure non-organic, composites, organic; solid/gel. Macrostructure 宏觀結構: External geometry congruent to tissue. Porosity 孔隙率: Isolated/interconnected 孤立/互連; improves nutrition. Pore sizes 孔徑 200-500μm for vascular ingrowth 血管長入. Tubular structures 管狀結構 beneficial for bone. High surface/volume ratio 表面積/體積比 for cell attachment. Mechanical strength 機械強度 for load-bearing 承重 (cartilage/bone 軟骨/骨). Degradation 降解 parallels tissue formation, retains integrity. No toxic products 有毒產物 released. Challenges: Control differentiation, porosity vs strength. Processing: Consistent technologies.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Porosity Calculation 孔隙率計算:Porosity (%) = (Void Volume 空隙體積 / Total Volume 總體積) x 100; Ideal >80%
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Isolated vs Interconnected Pores 孤立孔 vs 互連孔
Aspect 方面 Isolated Pores 孤立孔 Interconnected Pores 互連孔
Nutrition 營養 Limited diffusion 擴散有限 Enhanced flow 流動增強
Cell Survival 細胞存活 Poor in depths 深處差 Better in regions 區域性好
Applications 應用 Basic scaffolds 基礎支架 Advanced regeneration 高級再生
Vascularization 血管化 Low 低 High (200-500μm) 高 (200-500μm)
Strength 強度 Higher 較高 May compromise 可能降低
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 孔徑錯誤 (Pore size error): 混淆血管長入孔徑 (200-500μm) 與一般孔徑。 忽略比率 (Ignoring ratio): 陷阱題關於黏附而不提表面積/體積比。 降解毒性 (Degradation toxicity): 忘記無毒要求。
Topic 9 – Types of Scaffolds 支架類型大圖景 (Big Picture)Scaffolds are classified by biocompatibility 生物相容性 as bioinert 生物惰性 (minimal interaction 最小相互作用), bioactive 生物活性 (interacts with tissue 與組織相互作用), or bioresorbable 生物可吸收 (dissolves and replaced by tissue 溶解並被組織取代), and by material as natural 天然 or synthetic 合成. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Types 類型 → Biocompatibility 生物相容性 (Inert/Active/Resorbable 惰性/活性/可吸收) → Material 材料 (Natural/Synthetic 天然/合成) → Examples 例子 (Ti inert 鈦惰性, HA active 羥基磷灰石活性, TCP resorbable 磷酸三鈣可吸收) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Bioinert 生物惰性: Minimal tissue interaction (e.g., Titanium 鈦). Bioactive 生物活性: Bonds to bone/soft tissue (e.g., HA 羥基磷灰石). Bioresorbable 生物可吸收: Dissolves, replaced by tissue (e.g., Tricalcium phosphate (TCP) 磷酸三鈣). Classification of bioceramics 生物陶瓷: Inert (dental implants 牙科植入物), bioactive (HA coating HA塗層), surface active 表面活性 (bioglass 生物玻璃), resorbable (TCP). Natural 天然: Biologically active 生物活性, complex structure, high cost, pathogenic risks 致病風險. Synthetic 合成: Modifiable, cheap, reproducible, but degradation/biocompatibility concerns 降解/生物相容性問題. Natural examples: Collagen 膠原蛋白, agarose 瓊脂糖, heparin 肝素. Synthetic: Metals, ceramics, polymers. Natural suffice most criteria but limited modification. Synthetic enable specific properties and shapes. Bioinert: Fibrous capsule formation 纖維囊形成. Bioactive: Bonding within 30 days. Resorbable: Disappears 10-30 days. Composites 複合材料 combine fillers in matrix.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Biocompatibility Classes 生物相容性分類:Bioinert 生物惰性 → No Bond 無結合Bioactive 生物活性 → Bond (30 days) 結合 (30天)Bioresorbable 生物可吸收 → Resorb (10-30 days) 吸收 (10-30天)
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Bioinert vs Bioactive vs Bioresorbable
Aspect 方面 Bioinert 生物惰性 Bioactive 生物活性 Bioresorbable 生物可吸收
Interaction 相互作用 Minimal 最小 Bonds to tissue 與組織結合 Dissolves/replaced 溶解/被取代
Examples 例子 Titanium 鈦 Hydroxyapatite (HA) 羥基磷灰石 Tricalcium phosphate (TCP) 磷酸三鈣
Tissue Response 組織反應 Fibrous capsule 纖維囊 Bone ingrowth 骨長入 Tissue advancement 組織前進
Duration 持續時間 Permanent 永久 Stable bond 穩定結合 Temporary 暫時
Applications 應用 Stents, instruments 支架，器械 Coatings, restorations 塗層，修復體 Bone grafts 骨移植物
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 錯誤分類 (Misclassifying): 混淆生物活性與生物可吸收；考官用例子測試。 忽略風險 (Ignoring risks): 天然材料嘅致病性問題。 誇大合成材料 (Overstating synthetic): 忘記毒性問題。
Topic 10 – Scaffold Materials (Metals and Ceramics) 支架材料（金屬與陶瓷）大圖景 (Big Picture)Scaffold materials 支架材料 include metals 金屬 (e.g., Ti 鈦, Mg 鎂) for strength and ceramics 陶瓷 (e.g., HA 羥基磷灰石, bioglass 生物玻璃) for bioactivity, each with advantages in biocompatibility but limitations in toxicity or mechanics. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Materials 材料 → Metals 金屬 (Ti scaffolds 鈦支架, Mg alloys 鎂合金) → Ceramics 陶瓷 (CaP 磷酸鈣, HA, bioglass) → Properties 特性 (corrosion resistance 耐腐蝕性, resorbable 可吸收) → Applications 應用 (orthopedic 骨科, dental 牙科) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Metals 金屬: High strength, but lack biological recognition 生物識別; surface modification 表面改性 needed. Possible toxic ion release 有毒離子釋放 from corrosion/wear 腐蝕/磨損. Examples: 316L SS 316L不鏽鋼 for stents, Ti alloys for bone replacement. Ti 鈦: High strength, light, corrosion resistant 耐腐蝕, biocompatible; porous for fixation. Bioinert 生物惰性, slow bone bonding; BMP-loading BMP載藥 enhances. Mg alloys 鎂合金: Bioresorbable 生物可吸收, osteoconductive 骨傳導性, but rapid dissolution risk (hypermagnesia 高鎂血症). Ceramics 陶瓷: Refractory 耐火, inert, biocompatible; hard, brittle. CaP 磷酸鈣: Bioactive 生物活性, resorbable; poor mechanics, used as coatings. HA 羥基磷灰石: Similar to bone mineral, supports ingrowth; for augmentation 增補. Bioglass 生物玻璃: Bonds to tissues, composition SiO2-CaO-Na2O; bone cement 骨水泥. Bioactivity regions 生物活性區域: A (bonding 結合), B (inert 惰性), C (resorbable 可吸收), D (non-glass 非玻璃). Resorbable 可吸收: Replaced by bone without mechanical loss. Metals for hard tissues, polymers for soft. Challenges: Toxicity, corrosion for metals; brittleness 脆性 for ceramics.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)HA Formula HA化學式: Ca10(PO4)6(OH)2TCP Formula TCP化學式: Ca3(PO4)2
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Metals vs Ceramics 金屬 vs 陶瓷
Aspect 方面 Metals 金屬 Ceramics 陶瓷
Properties 特性 Strong, deformable 強，可變形 Hard, brittle 硬，脆
Biocompatibility 生物相容性 Bioinert, modifiable 生物惰性，可改性 Bioactive/resorbable 生物活性/可吸收
Examples 例子 Ti, Mg HA, bioglass
Limitations 局限性 Toxic ions, corrosion 有毒離子，腐蝕 Poor load-bearing 承載能力差
Applications 應用 Bone plates, stents 骨板，支架 Coatings, bone cement 塗層，骨水泥
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 忘記改性 (Forgetting modifications): 金屬需要塗層以獲得生物活性。 鎂毒性 (Mg toxicity): 陷阱題在提及吸收時不提及排泄/高鎂血症。 HA vs TCP (HA vs TCP): 混淆不可吸收嘅HA與可吸收嘅TCP。
Active Recall Bank 主動回憶庫 What are the four components of the tissue engineering paradigm? (Cells, Biocompatible Materials, Biochemical Factors, Biophysical Factors) Who championed the tensegrity model? (Don Ingber) Tension elements in tensegrity: (Actin filaments) Compression elements: (Microtubules) What is pre-stress in cells? (Contractile tension from actomyosin) Three main mechanoreceptors: (Integrins, Cadherins, Stretch-activated ion channels) Integrins link (ECM) to (cytoskeleton) Focal complexes evolve into (Focal Adhesions) Key pathway in mechanosignaling: FAK to (ERK) VEGF activates (Ras-Raf-MEK-ERK) pathway Cell shape changes switch programs for (apoptosis) or (growth/differentiation) Stiffness for neuron differentiation: (Soft, ~0.1-1 kPa) Scaffolds mimic (natural ECM) Scaffold requirements: 3D (porous interconnected network), biocompatible, (mechanical match) Ideal pore size for vascular ingrowth: (200-500 μm) Bioinert example: (Titanium) Bioactive: (Hydroxyapatite, HA) Bioresorbable: (Tricalcium phosphate, TCP) Natural materials risk: (Pathogenic contamination 病原體污染) Synthetic advantage: (Modifiability, reproducibility 可改性，可重現性)
15-Minute Mock Exam 15分鐘模擬考試 Explain the tensegrity model and its role in mechanotransduction.
The tensegrity model views cells as prestressed structures with actin (tension) and microtubules (compression), enabling rapid force transmission from surface to nucleus, linking physical stimuli to molecular events.
Describe focal adhesions and their formation.
Focal adhesions are mature integrin-based structures requiring tension; formed by ligand occupation, clustering, and force exertion, involving proteins like FAK and vinculin for signaling.
How do mechanical forces influence cell differentiation?
Forces alter shape and contractility; e.g., high contractility promotes osteogenesis, while stiffness directs lineages (soft: neuron, medium: muscle, hard: bone) via mechanotransduction.
What are the requirements for an ideal scaffold?
3D porous interconnected network, biocompatible/bioresorbable with matched degradation, suitable surface chemistry, and mechanical properties aligning with implantation site.
Compare natural and synthetic scaffold materials.
Natural: Biologically active but costly and risky; synthetic: Modifiable, cheap, but potential toxicity from degradation.
