15-Minute Mock Exam Explain the tensegrity model and its role in mechanotransduction.
The tensegrity model views cells as prestressed structures with actin (tension) and microtubules (compression), enabling rapid force transmission from surface to nucleus, linking physical stimuli to molecular events.
Describe focal adhesions and their formation.
Focal adhesions are mature integrin-based structures requiring tension; formed by ligand occupation, clustering, and force exertion, involving proteins like FAK and vinculin for signaling.
How do mechanical forces influence cell differentiation?
Forces alter shape and contractility; e.g., high contractility promotes osteogenesis, while stiffness directs lineages (soft: neuron, medium: muscle, hard: bone) via mechanotransduction.
What are the requirements for an ideal scaffold?
3D porous interconnected network, biocompatible/bioresorbable with matched degradation, suitable surface chemistry, and mechanical properties aligning with implantation site.
Compare natural and synthetic scaffold materials.
Natural: Biologically active but costly and risky; synthetic: Modifiable, cheap, but potential toxicity from degradation.
One-Page Cheat Sheet Summary
Section Key Points
Paradigm Cells + Materials + Biochemical (GFs) + Biophysical (loading) → Regeneration
Tensegrity Actin (tension) + Microtubules (compression) + Pre-stress → Rapid force propagation
Receptors Integrins (ECM), Cadherins (cell-cell), Ion channels (stretch-activated)
Signaling FAK/ERK from FAs; VEGF: Ras-Raf-MEK-ERK; Wnt: β-catenin nuclear
Cell Effects Shape → Viability/Diff (soft neuron, hard bone); Proliferation at edges
Scaffolds Porous (200-500μm), biocompatible, degrade match growth, mechanical site-match
Types Bioinert (Ti), Bioactive (HA), Bioresorbable (TCP)
Materials Natural (collagen, active); Synthetic (modifiable); Metals (Ti strong, Mg resorb); Ceramics (HA bone-like, brittle)

entry.title: BME 2104 Tissue Engineering – 6
entry.content:
BME 2104 Tissue Engineering – 6Lecture Overview 🔴 Polymer basics and definitions 聚合物基礎與定義 🟢 Polymerization mechanisms 聚合機制 🔵 Polymer structures and states in solids 聚合物固體結構與狀態 🟢 Thermal and crystallinity properties 熱性能與結晶性 🔵 Crosslinked networks and hydrogels 交聯網絡與水凝膠 🔴 Natural polymer scaffolds and examples 天然聚合物支架與例子 🟢 Synthetic polymer scaffolds and degradation 合成聚合物支架與降解 🔵 3D cell cultures vs 2D and applications 3D vs 2D 細胞培養與應用
Topic 1 – Introduction to Polymers 聚合物簡介大圖景 (Big Picture)Polymers 聚合物 are large molecules essential for tissue engineering scaffolds due to their versatility in mimicking ECM 細胞外基質. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Polymers 聚合物 → Natural 天然 (Wood/Rubber/DNA) → Synthetic 合成 (PE/PS/PEG) → Biomedical Suitability 生物醫學適用性 (Strength/Biodegradability/Fabrication 強度/生物降解性/加工性) → Disadvantages 缺點 (Deformation/Toxicity/Degradation 變形/毒性/降解) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Polymers revolutionized chemistry with major everyday impact (Lord Todd quote). ECM 細胞外基質 components like collagen 膠原蛋白, proteins 蛋白質, and GAGs 糖胺聚糖 are polymers. Suited for biomedical apps 生物醫學應用: low/high strength, protein attachment 蛋白質黏附 resistance/encouragement. Biodegradable 生物可降解 or permanent; fabricable into complex shapes. Disadvantages: lower strength than metals/ceramics, deform over time. May deteriorate during sterilization 滅菌 or degrade catastrophically in body. Release toxic byproducts 有毒副產物 possible. Natural examples: Wood 木材, Rubber 橡膠, Leather 皮革, Cotton 棉花, Wool 羊毛, Silk 絲綢, DNA, Proteins 蛋白質. Synthetic examples: Polystyrene (PS) 聚苯乙烯, PET 聚對苯二甲酸乙二醇酯, PE 聚乙烯, PEG 聚乙二醇, Kevlar 凱夫拉. Polymers in scaffolds mimic ECM for cell support.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Polymer Chain Example 聚合物鏈例子:Monomer 單體: H2C=CH2 (Ethylene 乙烯)Polymer 聚合物: -[CH2-CH2]-n (Polyethylene 聚乙烯)
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Natural vs Synthetic Polymers 天然 vs 合成聚合物
Natural Polymers 天然聚合物 Synthetic Polymers 合成聚合物
From nature (e.g., wood, DNA) 來源於自然界 (如木材，DNA) Man-made (e.g., PE, PS) 人造 (如PE，PS)
Biocompatible but variable 生物相容性好但性質多變 Consistent but may inflame 性質穩定但可能引起炎症
Often biodegradable 通常可生物降解 Tunable degradation 可調節降解性
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 混淆聚合物與單體 (Confusing polymers with monomers): 陷阱題要求舉例聚合物時不指定類型。 忽略缺點 (Overlooking disadvantages): 如體內產生有毒副產物。
Topic 2 – Polymerization Types 聚合類型大圖景 (Big Picture)Polymerization 聚合作用 forms chains via addition 加成聚合 (non-degradable) or step-growth 逐步聚合 (degradable via hydrolysis 水解). 思維導圖 (Mind map):Polymerization 聚合作用 → Addition 加成聚合 (Radicals/Ions 自由基/離子 → No labile bonds 無不穩定鍵) → Step-Growth 逐步聚合 (Condensation 縮合 → Ester bonds 酯鍵 → Hydrolysis 水解) → Examples 例子 (PE vs PLA/PGA/PLGA) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Addition 加成聚合: Double-bond monomers 雙鍵單體 like ethylene 乙烯, vinyl chloride 氯乙烯, styrene 苯乙烯, MMA 甲基丙烯酸甲酯. Via radicals 自由基 or ions 離子; no labile bonds, not degradable. Example: Ethylene → Polyethylene (PE) 聚乙烯. Step-growth 逐步聚合: Functional group reactions 官能團反應, condensation 縮合. Well-known: PLA 聚乳酸, PGA 聚乙醇酸, PLGA 聚乳酸-乙醇酸共聚物. Degrade via main chain ester hydrolysis 酯鍵水解. PLA from lactic acid 乳酸 condensation. PGA from glycolic acid 乙醇酸. PLGA copolymers tunable.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Addition 加成聚合: n H2C=CH2 → -[H2C-CH2]-nStep-Growth 逐步聚合: n HOCH(CH3)COOH → -[OCH(CH3)CO]-n + (n-1)H2O
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Addition vs Step-Growth Polymerization 加成 vs 逐步聚合
Addition Polymerization 加成聚合 Step-Growth Polymerization 逐步聚合
Double bonds break 雙鍵斷裂 Functional groups react 官能團反應
Non-degradable chains 鏈不可降解 Degradable via hydrolysis 可通過水解降解
Examples: PE, PVC 例子: PE，PVC Examples: PLA, PGA 例子: PLA，PGA
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 混淆加成聚合與逐步聚合 (Mixing addition and step-growth): 陷阱題將 PLA 分類為加成聚合（實際係逐步聚合）。 忘記逐步聚合嘅副產物 (Forgetting condensation byproduct in step-growth): 有水生成。
Topic 3 – Polymer Structures and States 聚合物結構與狀態大圖景 (Big Picture)Polymers exist as crystalline 結晶, amorphous 無定形, or semi-crystalline 半結晶, affecting melting 熔化 and glass transitions 玻璃化轉變. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Structures 結構 → Crystals 晶體 (Ordered/Lattice/Sharp Tm 有序/晶格/銳利Tm) → Amorphous 無定形 (No order/Tg 無序/Tg) → Polymers 聚合物 (Semi-crystalline/Folding/Stacking 半結晶/摺疊/堆疊) → States 狀態 (Rubber/Glass 橡膠態/玻璃態) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Crystals 晶體: Infinite 3D lattice 無限三維晶格, sharp Tm 熔點. Amorphous 無定形: No structure, sharp Tm. Small molecules 小分子: 100% crystalline or amorphous. Polymer crystalline: Intramolecular folding/stacking 分子內摺疊/堆疊. Semi-crystalline 半結晶: Never 100% crystalline, have Tm. Rubber/Glass 橡膠態/玻璃態: Frozen liquid 凍結液體, no long-range order. Tg 玻璃化轉變溫度: Below Tg, no cooperative rotation/translation 協同旋轉/平移. Transition: Rubbery to glassy hard state. Crystallization 結晶 favored by simple structures, small side groups. Linear, non-crosslinked polymers crystallize better. Block/alternating copolymers 嵌段/交替共聚物, slow cooling aid crystallization. Crystalline: Stronger, resistant to dissolution.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Solid States 固體狀態:- Crystalline 結晶: Ordered chains 有序鏈 → Tm- Amorphous 無定形: Random 無序 → Tg
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Crystalline vs Amorphous 結晶 vs 無定形
Crystalline 結晶 Amorphous 無定形
Ordered lattice 有序晶格 No order 無序
Sharp Tm 銳利熔點 Tg only 只有玻璃化轉變溫度
Stronger 更強 Flexible 更柔韌
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 假設聚合物100%結晶 (Assuming polymers are 100% crystalline): 陷阱題 - 聚合物是半結晶的。 混淆Tm同Tg (Confusing Tm and Tg)。
Topic 4 – Polymer Properties 聚合物特性大圖景 (Big Picture)Properties like crystallinity 結晶度, thermal behavior 熱行為, and side groups 側基 influence polymer performance in scaffolds. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Properties 特性 → Crystallinity 結晶度 (Favored by simplicity 有利於簡單結構) → Side Groups 側基 (Flexible lower Tg 柔韌側基降低Tg) → Thermal 熱性能 (Tg/Tm values) → Effects 影響 (Free volume/Motion 自由體積/運動) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Crystallization 結晶: Simple/small side groups, linear, non-network. Slow cooling for chain motion. Crystalline stronger/resistant. Flexible side groups: Tg decreases with length. Side chains rotate, increase free volume 自由體積, lower Tg. Thermal examples: Nylon 6,6 尼龍6,6 Tg 45°C Tm 267°C. UHMWPE 超高分子量聚乙烯 Tg -125°C Tm 140°C. Silicone 矽橡膠 Tg -123°C Tm -29°C. PMMA 聚甲基丙烯酸甲酯 Tg 105°C Tm 160°C. PDLLA 聚消旋乳酸 Tg 50°C amorphous. PCL 聚己內酯 Tg -60°C Tm 57°C. PGA 聚乙醇酸 Tg 35°C Tm 210°C.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Tg Trend Tg趨勢:Increasing side chain 側鏈增長: Tg ↓ (e.g., PMMA 105°C → Polydecyl MA -62°C)
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Short vs Long Side Groups 短 vs 長側基
Short Side Groups 短側基 Long Side Groups 長側基
Higher Tg 較高Tg Lower Tg 較低Tg
Less free volume 較少自由體積 More free volume 較多自由體積
Restricted motion 運動受限 Easier rotation 易於旋轉
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 認為長側基會提高Tg (Thinking longer sides increase Tg): 實際上會降低。 忽略側基對堆積嘅影響 (Ignoring side chain effects on packing)。
Topic 5 – Crosslinked Networks and Hydrogels 交聯網絡與水凝膠大圖景 (Big Picture)Crosslinks 交聯鍵 create networks that swell in water to form hydrogels 水凝膠 for cell environments. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Crosslinks 交聯鍵 (Covalent/H-bond/Entangle 共價鍵/氫鍵/纏結) → Networks 網絡 (Increased MW/Swell 分子量增加/溶脹) → Hydrogels 水凝膠 (Water-swollen/Balanced forces 水溶脹/平衡力) → Apps 應用 (Nutrients/Waste/Aqueous mimic 營養/廢物/水環境模擬) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Crosslinks: Covalent 共價鍵, H-bonding 氫鍵, entanglements 纏結. Increase MW 分子量, swell in solvents (hydrogel). Hydrogels 水凝膠: Water-swollen crosslinked structures. From monomers or chain associations. Why: Cells need aqueous for nutrients/waste. Prepared by crosslinking then swelling. PHEMA 聚羥基乙基甲基丙烯酸酯 common: Tissue-like water, degradation resistant. Apps: Contacts 隱形眼鏡, wounds 傷口, kidney membranes 腎膜. Swelling: Thermodynamic force 熱力學力 vs retractive 收縮力. Highly swollen: Cellulose 纖維素, PVA 聚乙烯醇, PEG. Moderately: PHEMA.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Hydrogel Formation 水凝膠形成:Polymer chains + Crosslinker 聚合物鏈 + 交聯劑 → Network 網絡 → Swell in H2O 在水中溶脹
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Solid Porous Scaffolds vs Hydrogel Scaffolds 固體多孔支架 vs 水凝膠支架
Solid Porous Scaffolds 固體多孔支架 Hydrogel Scaffolds 水凝膠支架
For bone (stable) 用於骨（穩定） For soft tissues (fill defects) 用於軟組織（填充缺損）
Degrade by hydrolysis 通過水解降解 Incorporate cells/bioactives easily 易於包埋細胞/生物活性因子
Non-dissolving 不溶解 Minimally invasive 微創
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 混淆交聯與聚合 (Confusing crosslinks with polymerization): 陷阱題 - 水凝膠不需要交聯（實際需要）。 忽略溶脹平衡 (Overlooking swelling balance)。
Topic 6 – Natural Polymer Scaffolds 天然聚合物支架大圖景 (Big Picture)Natural polymers from proteins 蛋白質/polysaccharides 多糖 provide biocompatible scaffolds but with variability risks. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Natural Scaffolds 天然支架 → Proteins 蛋白質 (Collagen/Elastin 膠原蛋白/彈性蛋白) → Polysaccharides 多糖 (GAGs/Hyaluronan 糖胺聚糖/透明質酸) → Sources 來源 (Plants/Algae/Animals/Microbes 植物/藻類/動物/微生物) → Crosslinking 交聯 (Chemical/Physical 化學/物理) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Proteins: Amino acid polymers, include collagen, elastin, laminin. DNA/RNA: Phosphate-ribose backbone, not for scaffolds. Scaffolds: Proteins, polysaccharides, combos. Sources: Collagen/fibrin/elastin (animals), soybean (plants), silk fibroin 絲素蛋白. Gelatin 明膠: Partial hydrolysis of collagen from skin/bones. Extraction: Reduce crosslinks, acid/water at temp. Acidic pH speeds conversion. Scaffolds: Crosslink via -OH/-NH2/-COOH/-SH. Chemical mod tricky in water. May alter solubility/conformation/activity 溶解性/構象/活性. Collagen: Triple-helical 三螺旋, ECM main, immunogenicity risk 免疫原性風險. Crosslink: Physical (moisture/heat/UV), chemical (glutaraldehyde 戊二醛). Formaldehyde 甲醛: Fixes tissue by crosslinking aminos. Polysaccharides: Monosaccharide polymers, glycosidic bonds 糖苷鍵. GAGs: Unbranched 無分支, repeating disaccharides 重複雙糖單位 (chondroitin 硫酸軟骨素 etc.).
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Collagen Crosslink 膠原蛋白交聯:Protein-NH2 + Glutaraldehyde → Crosslinked 交聯
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Proteins vs Polysaccharides 蛋白質 vs 多糖
Proteins 蛋白質 Polysaccharides 多糖
Amino acids 氨基酸 Monosaccharides 單糖
Collagen/Gelatin 膠原蛋白/明膠 GAGs/Hyaluronan 糖胺聚糖/透明質酸
Crosslink via groups 通過官能團交聯 Glycosidic bonds 糖苷鍵
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 認為DNA/RNA用作支架 (Thinking DNA/RNA used as scaffolds): 佢哋唔係。 忽略天然聚合物嘅免疫原性 (Ignoring immunogenicity in natural polymers)。
Topic 7 – Specific Natural Polymers 特定天然聚合物大圖景 (Big Picture)Specific naturals like hyaluronan 透明質酸, matrigel 基質膠, fibrin 纖維蛋白, alginate 藻酸鹽, chitosan 殼聚糖 offer unique TE properties. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Specifics 特定聚合物 → Hyaluronan 透明質酸 (Repair/Cartilage/Hydrophilic 修復/軟骨/親水) → Matrigel 基質膠 (ECM mix/Stem cells ECM混合物/幹細胞) → Fibrin 纖維蛋白 (Coagulation/Biocompatible 凝血/生物相容) → Alginate 藻酸鹽 (Algae/Ca crosslink 藻類/鈣交聯) → Chitosan 殼聚糖 (Arthropod/Cartilage 節肢動物/軟骨) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Hyaluronan 透明質酸: Polysaccharide in repair/ECM, needs peptides for attachment. Crosslink: Acrylated HA 丙烯酸化HA with ADH/EDC, then thiol-acrylate 硫醇-丙烯酸酯. RGD addition aids proliferation/migration. Matrigel 基質膠: EHS sarcoma secretion, laminin/collagen/GFs 層粘連蛋白/膠原蛋白/生長因子. For ESCs 胚胎幹細胞: Diluted maintains pluripotency 多能性, undiluted differentiates. Fibrin 纖維蛋白: From fibrinogen/thrombin 纖維蛋白原/凝血酶, aggregates to coagulate. Biocompatible, used as sealant 密封劑, but degrades by fibrinolysis 纖維蛋白溶解. Alginate 藻酸鹽: From algae, hydrophilic, G-blocks crosslink with Ca (egg-carton 蛋盒模型). M-blocks not involved. Chitosan 殼聚糖: From exoskeletons 外骨骼, like GAGs, for cartilage/bone.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Hyaluronan Crosslink 透明質酸交聯:HA + ADH/EDC → HA-ADH + NHS-AC → Acrylated 丙烯酸化 → Thiol conjugate 硫醇偶聯物
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Fibrin vs Alginate 纖維蛋白 vs 藻酸鹽
Fibrin 纖維蛋白 Alginate 藻酸鹽
From blood 來源於血液 From algae 來源於藻類
Enzymatic cleavage 酶促裂解 Ca crosslinking 鈣交聯
Degrades fast 降解快 Stable 穩定
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 忘記透明質酸需要修飾才能黏附細胞 (Forgetting hyaluronan needs modification for attachment). 混淆藻酸鹽嘅G區同M區 (Mixing G/M blocks in alginate)。
Topic 8 – Synthetic Polymer Scaffolds 合成聚合物支架大圖景 (Big Picture)Synthetics offer consistency over naturals, with tunable degradation 可調節降解 for TE. 思維導圖 (Mind map):Synthetics 合成聚合物 → Less issues 問題較少 (No allergens/Batch var 無過敏原/批次差異) → Examples 例子 (PLA/PGA/PLGA/PCL/PEOT/PBT) → Degradation 降解 (Bulk/Surface 整體/表面) → Apps 應用 (Injectable/Long-term 可注射/長期) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) Less byproducts, variations, immunogenicity than naturals. PLA 聚乳酸: Semicrystalline, brittle 脆, hydrophobic 疏水, degrades to lactic acid 乳酸. Copolymerize with PEG for solubility. PGA 聚乙醇酸: Crystalline, tough 堅韌, degrades to glycolic acid 乙醇酸, for sutures 縫合線. PLGA 聚乳酸-乙醇酸共聚物: Copolymers, biocompatible, acidic products inflame. PCL 聚己內酯: Biocompatible, slow bulk erosion 整體侵蝕 (>3 yrs). PEOT/PBT: Elastic, reversible crosslinks, tunable properties. PPEs/PPAs 聚磷酸酯/聚磷酸酯膠: Surface erosion 表面侵蝕, milder response. PPF-DA/PEG-DA 聚富馬酸丙二醇酯-二丙烯酸酯/聚乙二醇-二丙烯酸酯: Injectable, fill defects, low strength. Categories: Porous solid (bone) vs hydrogels (soft tissues). Solid: Stable, non-dissolve, hydrolyze. Hydrogels: Fill defects, minimally invasive, incorporate cells.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)PLA Degradation PLA降解:-[OCH(CH3)CO]-n + H2O → Lactic acid 乳酸
對比表 (Comparison Table) - Natural vs Synthetic Polymers (Scaffolds) 天然 vs 合成聚合物 (支架)
Natural 天然 Synthetic 合成
Variable/biocompatible 多變/生物相容 Consistent/tunable 穩定/可調節
Immunogenic risk 免疫原性風險 Less inflammation 炎症較少
From sources 來源於天然 Lab-made 實驗室製備
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 認為合成聚合物不可降解 (Thinking synthetics non-biodegradable): 許多是可降解的。 忘記酸性降解產物 (Forgetting acidic degradation products): 可能引起炎症。
Topic 9 – 3D Cell Cultures and Applications 3D 細胞培養與應用大圖景 (Big Picture)3D cultures 3D培養 better mimic in vivo 體內 via scaffolds, improving cell behavior over 2D 二維. 思維導圖 (Mind map):3D vs 2D → Why 原因 (ECM signals/Topography ECM信號/拓撲結構) → Limitations 局限性 (Diffusion 擴散) → Apps 應用 (Regen med/Drug discovery/Bioreactors 再生醫學/藥物發現/生物反應器) 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) 2D artificial 人造; 3D natural with ECM cues. Control morphology/architecture/components. Cells respond in vivo-like. Matrices: Porous, support growth/differentiation. Chemical mod: Adhesion/degradation/GF release. Limited diffusion 擴散 in 3D; viability drops over time. Apps: Clinical 臨床, in vitro 體外, regen med 再生醫學, drug discovery 藥物發現. Bioreactors 生物反應器: Stirring/perfusion 攪拌/灌注 for mass transport 物質傳輸. Focal adhesions 黏著斑 differ 2D vs 3D. 3D for blood vessels/skin/cartilage 血管/皮膚/軟骨.
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Viability in 3D 3D培養中的活力:Day 1: 90% → Week 1: 60% → Week 3: <10%
對比表 (Comparison Table) - 2D vs 3D Cultures
2D Cultures 2D培養 3D Cultures 3D培養
Flat/rigid 平坦/剛性 Dynamic ECM 動態ECM
Misrepresent 不能準確代表 In vivo-like 更接近體內
Simple 簡單 Complex scaffolds 複雜支架
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 假設2D培養足夠 (Assuming 2D sufficient): 陷阱題 - 為何3D更好。 忘記大型組織需要生物反應器 (Forgetting bioreactor need for large tissues)。
Active Recall Bank 主動回憶庫 What is a polymer? (Large molecule made of repeating monomer units) Name two polymerization types: (Addition) and (Step-growth) Tg stands for: (Glass transition temperature) Factor favoring crystallinity: (Simple) structures Hydrogels are (Water)-swollen networks Natural polymer example: (Collagen) Gelatin from: (Partial) hydrolysis of collagen Hyaluronan needs (Peptides) for cell attachment PLA degrades to: (Lactic) acid PGA used for: (Sutures) Synthetic advantage over natural: No (batch) variations Crosslinks types: Covalent, (H-bonding), entanglements Matrigel for: (Embryonic) stem cells Fibrin from: (Fibrinogen) + thrombin Alginate crosslinks with: (Calcium) ions Chitosan from: (Arthropod) exoskeletons 3D cultures mimic: (In vivo) environments Limitation in 3D: Limited (diffusion) Bioreactors provide: (Mass) transport Focal adhesions differ in: (2D) vs (3D)
15-Minute Mock Exam 15分鐘模擬考試 Describe two common techniques for fabricating polymeric scaffolds in tissue engineering.
Examples include electrospinning 靜電紡絲 and solvent casting/particle leaching 溶劑鑄造/粒子浸出; these may not suit ceramics due to material differences.
Compare advantages and limitations of natural vs synthetic polymers for scaffolds.
Natural: Biocompatible, mimic ECM but variable/immunogenic; Synthetic: Consistent, tunable degradation but may cause inflammation from byproducts.
What properties make an ideal scaffold for tissue engineering?
Porous 多孔, biocompatible 生物相容, biodegradable at controlled rate 可控速率降解, mechanically suitable 機械性能合適, supports cell attachment/growth 支持細胞黏附/生長.
Explain the role of hydrogels in scaffold-based tissue engineering.
Hydrogels provide hydrated 3D environments mimicking soft tissues, allowing cell encapsulation 細胞封裝 and nutrient diffusion.
Which polymer is commonly used for absorbable sutures and why?
PGA; crystalline, tough, degrades to natural metabolite glycolic acid via hydrolysis.
How does crystallinity affect polymer properties in scaffolds?
Increases strength and resistance to dissolution 耐溶解; favored by simple linear structures and slow cooling.
Describe the degradation mechanism for PLGA copolymers.
Hydrolysis of ester bonds 酯鍵 in the main chain, releasing acidic products that may induce inflammation.
Why are bioreactors used in 3D tissue engineering?
To provide convective mass transport 對流物質傳輸 (nutrients/oxygen) via stirring/perfusion for larger-scale tissue development.
One-Page Cheat Sheet Summary 一頁速查表總結
Category 類別 Key Points 要點 (中英對照)
Polymers Basics 聚合物基礎 Natural 天然 (DNA/Collagen), Synthetic 合成 (PLA/PE); Suited for scaffolds: Versatile, but deform/toxic. 適用於支架：多功能，但會變形/有毒。
Polymerization 聚合 Addition 加成 (PE, non-degradable), Step-growth 逐步 (PLA/PGA, hydrolyze esters).
Structures/States 結構/狀態 Crystalline 結晶 (Tm), Amorphous 無定形 (Tg); Semi-crystalline 半結晶 polymers; Tg decreases with long sides.
Properties 特性 Crystallinity 結晶度: Simple/linear favors; Thermal 熱性能: e.g., PGA Tg 35°C Tm 210°C.
Networks/Hydrogels 網絡/水凝膠 Crosslinks swell; PHEMA common; Balance swelling/retractive forces. 交聯溶脹；PHEMA常用；平衡溶脹/收縮力。
Natural Scaffolds 天然支架 Collagen 膠原蛋白 (crosslink glutaraldehyde), Gelatin 明膠 (hydrolysis), Hyaluronan 透明質酸 (acrylate), Fibrin 纖維蛋白 (thrombin), Alginate 藻酸鹽 (Ca), Chitosan 殼聚糖 (GAG-like).
Synthetic Scaffolds 合成支架 PLA (brittle 脆), PGA (sutures 縫合線), PCL (slow degrade 慢降解), Injectables 可注射材料 (PPF-DA).
3D Cultures 3D培養 Vs 2D: In vivo-like; Limitations: Diffusion; Apps: Regen med, bioreactors for transport. 對比2D：更似體內；局限性：擴散；應用：再生醫學，生物反應器用於物質傳輸。
English Version BelowTopic 1 – Introduction to PolymersBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Polymers are large molecules essential for tissue engineering scaffolds due to their versatility in mimicking ECM.Mind map: Polymers → Natural (Wood/Rubber/DNA) → Synthetic (PE/PS/PEG) → Biomedical Suitability (Strength/Biodegradability/Fabrication) → Disadvantages (Deformation/Toxicity/Degradation). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) Polymers revolutionized chemistry with major everyday impact (Lord Todd quote). ECM components like collagen, proteins, and GAGs are polymers. Suited for biomedical apps: low/high strength, protein attachment resistance/encouragement. Biodegradable or permanent; fabricable into complex shapes. Disadvantages: lower strength than metals/ceramics, deform over time. May deteriorate during sterilization or degrade catastrophically in body. Release toxic byproducts possible. Natural examples: Wood, Rubber, Leather, Cotton, Wool, Silk, DNA, Proteins. Synthetic examples: Polystyrene, PET, PE, PEG, Kevlar. Polymers in scaffolds mimic ECM for cell support.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Polymer Chain Example:Monomer: H2C=CH2 (Ethylene)Polymer: -[CH2-CH2]-n (Polyethylene) Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Natural Polymers Synthetic Polymers
From nature (e.g., wood, DNA) Man-made (e.g., PE, PS)
Biocompatible but variable Consistent but may inflame
Often biodegradable Tunable degradation
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Confusing polymers with monomers; trap: asking for polymer examples without specifying type. Overlooking disadvantages like toxic byproducts in body.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks “Poly-Many”: Polymers are many monomers linked (poly = many).
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)What are the advantages of polymers in biomedical applications?
Versatile strength, protein attachment control, biodegradability, and easy fabrication into shapes.
Name two disadvantages of polymers.
Lower strength/deformation over time; potential toxic degradation products.
Topic 2 – Polymerization TypesBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Polymerization forms chains via addition (non-degradable) or step-growth (degradable via hydrolysis).Mind map: Polymerization → Addition (Radicals/Ions → No labile bonds) → Step-Growth (Condensation → Ester bonds → Hydrolysis) → Examples (PE vs PLA/PGA/PLGA). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) Addition: Double-bond monomers like ethylene, vinyl chloride, styrene, MMA. Via radicals or ions; no labile bonds, not degradable. Example: Ethylene → Polyethylene. Step-growth: Functional group reactions, condensation. Well-known: PLA, PGA, PLGA. Degrade via main chain ester hydrolysis. PLA from lactic acid condensation. PGA from glycolic acid. PLGA copolymers tunable.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Addition: n H2C=CH2 → -[H2C-CH2]-nStep-Growth: n HOCH(CH3)COOH → -[OCH(CH3)CO]-n + (n-1)H2O Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Addition Polymerization Step-Growth Polymerization
Double bonds break Functional groups react
Non-degradable chains Degradable via hydrolysis
Examples: PE, PVC Examples: PLA, PGA
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Mixing addition and step-growth; trap: classify PLA as addition (it’s step-growth). Forgetting condensation byproduct in step-growth.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks “Add No Water”: Addition has no H2O byproduct; “Step Condense”: Step-growth condenses with H2O loss.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Describe addition polymerization.
Involves double-bond monomers reacting via radicals/ions, forming non-degradable polymers.
Why do step-growth polymers degrade?
Hydrolysis of main chain ester bonds.
Topic 3 – Polymer Structures and StatesBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Polymers exist as crystalline, amorphous, or semi-crystalline, affecting melting and glass transitions.Mind map: Structures → Crystals (Ordered/Lattice/Sharp Tm) → Amorphous (No order/Tg) → Polymers (Semi-crystalline/Folding/Stacking) → States (Rubber/Glass). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) Crystals: Infinite 3D lattice, sharp Tm. Amorphous: No structure, sharp Tm. Small molecules: 100% crystalline or amorphous. Polymer crystalline: Intramolecular folding/stacking. Semi-crystalline: Never 100% crystalline, have Tm. Rubber/Glass: Frozen liquid, no long-range order. Tg: Below Tg, no cooperative rotation/translation. Transition: Rubbery to glassy hard state. Crystallization favored by simple structures, small side groups. Linear, non-crosslinked polymers crystallize better. Block/alternating copolymers, slow cooling aid crystallization. Crystalline: Stronger, resistant to dissolution.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Solid States:- Crystalline: Ordered chains → Tm- Amorphous: Random → TgDiagram:Crystalline: |||||| (aligned)Amorphous: ~ ~ ~ (tangled) Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Crystalline Amorphous
Ordered lattice No order
Sharp Tm Tg only
Stronger Flexible
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Assuming polymers are 100% crystalline; trap: polymers are semi-crystalline. Confusing Tm and Tg.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks “Crystal Clear Tm”: Crystals have sharp melting; “Amorphous Tg”: Amorphous has glass transition.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)What is Tg?
Glass transition temperature where polymer shifts from rubbery to glassy.
Factors favoring crystallization?
Simple structures, linear chains, slow cooling.
Topic 4 – Polymer PropertiesBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Properties like crystallinity, thermal behavior, and side groups influence polymer performance in scaffolds.Mind map: Properties → Crystallinity (Favored by simplicity) → Side Groups (Flexible lower Tg) → Thermal (Tg/Tm values) → Effects (Free volume/Motion). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) Crystallization: Simple/small side groups, linear, non-network. Slow cooling for chain motion. Crystalline stronger/resistant. Flexible side groups: Tg decreases with length. Side chains rotate, increase free volume, lower Tg. Thermal examples: Nylon 6,6 Tg 45°C Tm 267°C. UHMWPE Tg -125°C Tm 140°C. Silicone Tg -123°C Tm -29°C. PMMA Tg 105°C Tm 160°C. PDLLA Tg 50°C amorphous. PCL Tg -60°C Tm 57°C. PGA Tg 35°C Tm 210°C.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Tg Trend:Increasing side chain: Tg ↓ (e.g., PMMA 105°C → Polydecyl MA -62°C) Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Short Side Groups Long Side Groups
Higher Tg Lower Tg
Less free volume More free volume
Restricted motion Easier rotation
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Thinking longer sides increase Tg; actually decrease. Ignoring side chain effects on packing.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks “Long Sides Low Tg”: Longer flexible groups lower transition.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)How do side groups affect Tg?
Flexible long sides lower Tg by increasing free volume.
Give Tg and Tm for PGA.
Tg 35°C, Tm 210°C.
Topic 5 – Crosslinked Networks and HydrogelsBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Crosslinks create networks that swell in water to form hydrogels for cell environments.Mind map: Crosslinks (Covalent/H-bond/Entangle) → Networks (Increased MW/Swell) → Hydrogels (Water-swollen/Balanced forces) → Apps (Nutrients/Waste/Aqueous mimic). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) Crosslinks: Covalent, H-bonding, entanglements. Increase MW, swell in solvents (hydrogel). Hydrogels: Water-swollen crosslinked structures. From monomers or chain associations. Why: Cells need aqueous for nutrients/waste. Prepared by crosslinking then swelling. PHEMA common: Tissue-like water, degradation resistant. Apps: Contacts, wounds, kidney membranes. Swelling: Thermodynamic force vs retractive. Highly swollen: Cellulose, PVA, PEG. Moderately: PHEMA.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Hydrogel Formation:Polymer chains + Crosslinker → Network → Swell in H2O Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Solid Porous Scaffolds Hydrogel Scaffolds
For bone (stable) For soft tissues (fill defects)
Degrade by hydrolysis Incorporate cells/bioactives easily
Non-dissolving Minimally invasive
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Confusing crosslinks with polymerization; trap: hydrogels don’t need crosslinks (they do). Overlooking swelling balance.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks “Cross Swell Gel”: Crosslinks lead to swelling in hydrogels.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)What are hydrogels?
Water-swollen crosslinked polymeric structures.
Why use hydrogels in TE?
Mimic aqueous cell environment for nutrient/waste exchange.
Topic 6 – Natural Polymer ScaffoldsBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Natural polymers from proteins/polysaccharides provide biocompatible scaffolds but with variability risks.Mind map: Natural Scaffolds → Proteins (Collagen/Elastin) → Polysaccharides (GAGs/Hyaluronan) → Sources (Plants/Algae/Animals/Microbes) → Crosslinking (Chemical/Physical). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) Proteins: Amino acid polymers, include collagen, elastin, laminin. DNA/RNA: Phosphate-ribose backbone, not for scaffolds. Scaffolds: Proteins, polysaccharides, combos. Sources: Collagen/fibrin/elastin (animals), soybean (plants), silk fibroin. Gelatin: Partial hydrolysis of collagen from skin/bones. Extraction: Reduce crosslinks, acid/water at temp. Acidic pH speeds conversion. Scaffolds: Crosslink via -OH/-NH2/-COOH/-SH. Chemical mod tricky in water. May alter solubility/conformation/activity. Collagen: Triple-helical, ECM main, immunogenicity risk. Crosslink: Physical (moisture/heat/UV), chemical (glutaraldehyde). Formaldehyde: Fixes tissue by crosslinking aminos. Polysaccharides: Monosaccharide polymers, glycosidic bonds. GAGs: Unbranched, repeating disaccharides (chondroitin etc.).
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Collagen Crosslink:Protein-NH2 + Glutaraldehyde → Crosslinked Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Proteins Polysaccharides
Amino acids Monosaccharides
Collagen/Gelatin GAGs/Hyaluronan
Crosslink via groups Glycosidic bonds
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Thinking DNA/RNA used as scaffolds; they’re not. Ignoring immunogenicity in natural polymers.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks “Natural Cross Groups”: -OH/-NH2/-COOH/-SH for crosslinking.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Sources of natural polymers?
Plants (soybean), algae (alginate), animals (collagen), microbes (dextran).
How extract gelatin?
Partial hydrolysis of collagen in acid/water at increasing temps.
Topic 7 – Specific Natural PolymersBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Specific naturals like hyaluronan, matrigel, fibrin, alginate, chitosan offer unique TE properties.Mind map: Specifics → Hyaluronan (Repair/Cartilage/Hydrophilic) → Matrigel (ECM mix/Stem cells) → Fibrin (Coagulation/Biocompatible) → Alginate (Algae/Ca crosslink) → Chitosan (Arthropod/Cartilage). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) Hyaluronan: Polysaccharide in repair/ECM, needs peptides for attachment. Crosslink: Acrylated HA with ADH/EDC, then thiol-acrylate. RGD addition aids proliferation/migration. Matrigel: EHS sarcoma secretion, laminin/collagen/GFs. For ESCs: Diluted maintains pluripotency, undiluted differentiates. Fibrin: From fibrinogen/thrombin, aggregates to coagulate. Biocompatible, used as sealant, but degrades by fibrinolysis. Alginate: From algae, hydrophilic, G-blocks crosslink with Ca (egg-carton). M-blocks not involved. Chitosan: From exoskeletons, like GAGs, for cartilage/bone.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Hyaluronan Crosslink:HA + ADH/EDC → HA-ADH + NHS-AC → Acrylated → Thiol conjugate Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Fibrin Alginate
From blood From algae
Enzymatic cleavage Ca crosslinking
Degrades fast Stable
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Forgetting hyaluronan needs modification for attachment. Mixing G/M blocks in alginate.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks “Hyal Needs Help”: Hyaluronan requires peptides for cell attach.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)How crosslink hyaluronan?
Acrylate with ADH/EDC, then thiol-acrylate.
What is matrigel for?
Maintain ESC pluripotency (diluted) or differentiation (undiluted).
Topic 8 – Synthetic Polymer ScaffoldsBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Synthetics offer consistency over naturals, with tunable degradation for TE.Mind map: Synthetics → Less issues (No allergens/Batch var) → Examples (PLA/PGA/PLGA/PCL/PEOT/PBT) → Degradation (Bulk/Surface) → Apps (Injectable/Long-term). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) Less byproducts, variations, immunogenicity than naturals. PLA: Semicrystalline, brittle, hydrophobic, degrades to lactic acid. Copolymerize with PEG for solubility. PGA: Crystalline, tough, degrades to glycolic acid, for sutures. PLGA: Copolymers, biocompatible, acidic products inflame. PCL: Biocompatible, slow bulk erosion (>3 yrs). PEOT/PBT: Elastic, reversible crosslinks, tunable properties. PPEs/PPAs: Surface erosion, milder response. PPF-DA/PEG-DA: Injectable, fill defects, low strength. Categories: Porous solid (bone) vs hydrogels (soft tissues). Solid: Stable, non-dissolve, hydrolyze. Hydrogels: Fill defects, minimally invasive, incorporate cells.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)PLA Degradation:-[OCH(CH3)CO]-n + H2O → Lactic acid Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
Natural Synthetic
Variable/biocompatible Consistent/tunable
Immunogenic risk Less inflammation
From sources Lab-made
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Thinking synthetics non-biodegradable; many are. Forgetting acidic degradation products.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks “Synthetic Safe”: Less risks than natural.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Advantages of synthetics?
No byproducts/variations/immunogenicity.
Describe PCL.
Good biocompatibility, slow bulk erosion for long-term implants.
Topic 9 – 3D Cell Cultures and ApplicationsBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)3D cultures better mimic in vivo via scaffolds, improving cell behavior over 2D.Mind map: 3D vs 2D → Why (ECM signals/Topography) → Limitations (Diffusion) → Apps (Regen med/Drug discovery/Bioreactors). Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) 2D artificial; 3D natural with ECM cues. Control morphology/architecture/components. Cells respond in vivo-like. Matrices: Porous, support growth/differentiation. Chemical mod: Adhesion/degradation/GF release. Limited diffusion in 3D; viability drops over time. Apps: Clinical, in vitro, regen med, drug discovery. Bioreactors: Stirring/perfusion for mass transport. Focal adhesions differ 2D vs 3D. 3D for blood vessels/skin/cartilage.
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Viability in 3D:Day 1: 90% → Week 1: 60% → Week 3: <10% Comparison Table (“X vs Y” in proper Markdown table)
2D Cultures 3D Cultures
Flat/rigid Dynamic ECM
Misrepresent In vivo-like
Simple Complex scaffolds
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Assuming 2D sufficient; trap: why 3D better. Forgetting bioreactor need for large tissues.
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks “3D Real Deal”: 3D mimics real tissue better.
Past Exam Questions + Model Answers (use > blockquote for answers)Why 3D over 2D?
Reproduces complex environments, better cell responses.
Role of bioreactors?
Provide mass transport for cm-scale tissues.
Active Recall Bank What is a polymer? ____ Name two polymerization types: ____ and ____ Tg stands for: ____ Factor favoring crystallinity: ____ structures Hydrogels are ____-swollen networks Natural polymer example: ____ Gelatin from: ____ hydrolysis Hyaluronan needs ____ for cell attachment PLA degrades to: ____ acid PGA used for: ____ Synthetic advantage over natural: No ____ variations Crosslinks types: Covalent, ____, entanglements Matrigel for: ____ stem cells Fibrin from: ____ + thrombin Alginate crosslinks with: ____ ions Chitosan from: ____ exoskeletons 3D cultures mimic: ____ environments Limitation in 3D: Limited ____ Bioreactors provide: ____ transport Focal adhesions differ in: ____ vs ____
15-Minute Mock Exam Describe two common techniques for fabricating polymeric scaffolds in tissue engineering.
Examples include electrospinning and solvent casting/particle leaching; these may not suit ceramics due to material differences.
Compare advantages and limitations of natural vs synthetic polymers for scaffolds.
Natural: Biocompatible, mimic ECM but variable/immunogenic; Synthetic: Consistent, tunable degradation but may cause inflammation from byproducts.
What properties make an ideal scaffold for tissue engineering?
Porous, biocompatible, biodegradable at controlled rate, mechanically suitable, supports cell attachment/growth.
Explain the role of hydrogels in scaffold-based tissue engineering.
Hydrogels provide hydrated 3D environments mimicking soft tissues, allowing cell encapsulation and nutrient diffusion.
Which polymer is commonly used for absorbable sutures and why?
PGA; crystalline, tough, degrades to natural metabolite glycolic acid via hydrolysis.
How does crystallinity affect polymer properties in scaffolds?
Increases strength and resistance to dissolution; favored by simple linear structures and slow cooling.
Describe the degradation mechanism for PLGA copolymers.
Hydrolysis of ester bonds in the main chain, releasing acidic products that may induce inflammation.
Why are bioreactors used in 3D tissue engineering?
To provide convective mass transport (nutrients/oxygen) via stirring/perfusion for larger-scale tissue development.
One-Page Cheat Sheet Summary
Category Key Points
Polymers Basics Natural (DNA/Collagen), Synthetic (PLA/PE); Suited for scaffolds: Versatile, but deform/toxic.
Polymerization Addition (PE, non-degradable); Step-growth (PLA/PGA, hydrolyze esters).
Structures/States Crystalline (Tm), Amorphous (Tg); Semi-crystalline polymers; Tg decreases with long sides.
Properties Crystallinity: Simple/linear favors; Thermal: e.g., PGA Tg 35°C Tm 210°C.
Networks/Hydrogels Crosslinks swell; PHEMA common; Balance swelling/retractive forces.
Natural Scaffolds Collagen (crosslink glutaraldehyde), Gelatin (hydrolysis), Hyaluronan (acrylate), Fibrin (thrombin), Alginate (Ca), Chitosan (GAG-like).
Synthetic Scaffolds PLA (brittle), PGA (sutures), PCL (slow degrade), Injectables (PPF-DA).
3D Cultures Vs 2D: In vivo-like; Limitations: Diffusion; Apps: Regen med, bioreactors for transport.

entry.title: BME 2104 Tissue Engineering – 7
entry.content:
BME 2104 Tissue Engineering – Lecture 7: Scaffold Foundations (III) Final Revision NotesLecture Overview 🔴 Top 8 high-yield topics 八大高頻考點 that appear every year: Scaffold fabrication techniques 支架製備技術 & porosity creation 孔隙形成 Advantages/disadvantages of classic methods 傳統方法優缺點 (solvent casting-PL, gas foaming, freeze-drying, etc.) Advanced biofabrication 先進生物製造技術 (electrospinning 靜電紡絲, 3D printing, decellularization 脫細胞) Hydrogels 水凝膠 & cell encapsulation 細胞封裝 Limitations of conventional scaffolds 傳統支架局限性 Bioreactors 生物反應器 & cultivation strategies 培養策略 Host response to implanted scaffolds 宿主對植入支架的反應 (inflammation → fibrosis 炎症 → 纖維化) Immunoisolation 免疫隔離 & encapsulated cell therapy 封裝細胞療法
Topic 1 – Scaffold Fabrication Principles & Material Selection 支架製備原則與材料選擇大圖景 (Big Picture)Scaffold must mimic native ECM 細胞外基質 mechanically and biologically → material choice dictates fabrication method → porosity 孔隙率 & interconnectivity 互連性 are critical for cell migration 細胞遷移/nutrient diffusion 營養擴散. Material 材料 → Soft tissue 軟組織 (polymers 聚合物) vs Hard tissue 硬組織 (ceramics 陶瓷/metals 金屬/composites 複合材料) ↓ Fabrication method 製備方法 → Porosity technique 造孔技術 ↓ Final scaffold properties 最終支架特性
必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) 🔴 Soft tissues 軟組織 (skin 皮膚, tendon 肌腱, liver 肝臟, vessels 血管) → synthetic/natural polymers 合成/天然聚合物 🔴 Hard tissues 硬組織 (bone 骨) → metals 金屬, ceramics 陶瓷, polymer-containing composites 含聚合物複合材料 🔴 Scaffold must have high porosity 高孔隙率 (>90% ideal 理想>90%), large surface area 大表面積, interconnected pores 互連孔隙 🔴 Pore size 孔徑: 100–500 μm generally optimal for most tissues 🟢 Fabrication technique depends on polymer solubility 聚合物溶解性, Tg/Tm, biocompatibility needs 生物相容性需求 🔴 All methods aim to create 3D porous structure 3D多孔結構 for cell infiltration 細胞浸潤 & vascularisation 血管化
關鍵公式/圖表 (Key Formulas / Diagrams)Porosity (%) = [1 – (ρscaffold / ρbulk polymer)] × 100孔隙率 (%) = [1 – (支架密度 / 本體聚合物密度)] × 100
對比表 (Comparison Table)
Property 特性 Soft Tissue Scaffolds 軟組織支架 Hard Tissue Scaffolds 硬組織支架
Main materials 主要材料 PLGA, PLLA, collagen 膠原蛋白, alginate 藻酸鹽 HA 羥基磷灰石, β-TCP, Ti鈦, composites 複合材料
Mechanical need 機械需求 Elastic 彈性, flexible 柔韌 High compressive strength 高抗壓強度
Typical fabrication 典型製備 Polymer-based (foaming發泡, electrospinning靜電紡絲) Ceramic sintering陶瓷燒結, metal mesh金屬網
常見錯誤同考官陷阱 (Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps) 忘記陶瓷單獨用於軟組織承重時太脆 (Forgetting that ceramics alone are too brittle for load-bearing soft tissue) 以為金屬總係用於軟組織 (Thinking metals are always used for soft tissue) - 實際上唔係
Topic 2 – Conventional Porosity Techniques 傳統造孔技術大圖景 (Big Picture)Five classic ways to make pores; each has trade-offs in interconnectivity, solvent use, and pore control. 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) 🔴 Solvent-casting + particulate leaching (SCPL) 溶劑鑄造+粒子浸出 – gold standard 金標準, uses salt/sugar, highly interconnected 🔴 Gas foaming 氣體發泡 – no organic solvent 無有機溶劑 (advantage), but mostly closed pores 閉孔 🔴 Gas foaming + particulate leaching 氣體發泡+粒子浸出 – solves interconnectivity problem 🔴 Melt molding 熔融成型 – no solvent, uses heat + porogen 致孔劑 (e.g., gelatin particles) 🔴 Phase separation 相分離 – low temperature, good for incorporating growth factors 生長因子 🔴 Freeze-drying 冷凍乾燥 – emulsion or non-emulsion, pore size controlled by freezing rate/pH
關鍵圖表 (Key Diagrams)SCPL Process SCPL過程:Polymer + salt in solvent 溶劑中的聚合物+鹽 → cast 鑄模 → evaporate solvent 蒸發溶劑 → leach salt with water 用水浸出鹽 → porous scaffold 多孔支架
對比表 (Comparison Table)
Method 方法 Solvent? 使用溶劑? Interconnected pores? 互連孔隙? Pore control 孔徑控制 Bioactive molecule friendly? 對生物活性分子友好?
SCPL Yes Excellent 優秀 Good 良好 No (solvent)
Gas foaming No Poor (closed) 差 (閉孔) Poor 差 Yes
Gas foaming + PL No Excellent 優秀 Good 良好 Yes
Melt molding No Good 良好 Good 良好 No (high T)
Phase separation Yes Excellent 優秀 Good 良好 Yes (low T)
Freeze-drying Yes/No Excellent 優秀 Excellent 優秀 Yes
常見錯誤 (Common Mistakes) 話氣體發泡單獨使用能提供高互連性 (Saying gas foaming alone gives high interconnectivity) - 實際上唔得 忘記聚合物必須不溶於水但致孔劑必須水溶 (Forgetting polymer must be insoluble in water but porogen must be water-soluble)
Topic 3 – Advanced Biofabrication Techniques 先進生物製造技術大圖景 (Big Picture)Move from random porosity to precise, reproducible 3D architecture 3D結構 + ability to include cells during printing. 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) 🔴 Electrospinning 靜電紡絲 → nanofibers 納米纖維, excellent for ECM mimicry, cheap & fast 🔴 Rapid prototyping 快速原型 / 3D printing 3D打印 / microstereolithography 微立體光刻 → CAD-CAM control CAD-CAM控制 of exact geometry 🔴 Microsphere sintering 微球燒結 → trabecular bone-like pores 小梁骨樣孔隙 🔴 Decellularized organs 脫細胞器官 → keep native vascular network 血管網絡 (Ott 2008, 2010 – perfused lung 灌注肺) 🟢 Decellularization + recellularization 脫細胞+再細胞化 = whole organ engineering gold standard 全器官工程金標準
對比表 (Comparison Table)
Technique 技術 Resolution 分辨率 Speed 速度 Cell inclusion during fab? 製造時包含細胞? Vascular network? 血管網絡?
Electrospinning 靜電紡絲 Nano 納米 Fast 快 No 否 No 否
3D Bioprinting 3D生物打印 50–100 μm Slow 慢 Yes (bioink 生物墨水) Possible 可能
Decellularization 脫細胞 Native 原生 Slow 慢 No (recell later 稍後再細胞化) Yes (native 原生)
考官陷阱 (Examiner Trap)學生忘記脫細胞支架已經擁有完美嘅血管樹 (Students forget decellularized scaffolds already have perfect vascular tree) Topic 4 – Hydrogels & Cell Encapsulation 水凝膠與細胞封裝大圖景 (Big Picture)Hydrogels 水凝膠 = water-swollen polymer networks 水溶脹聚合物網絡; unique ability to encapsulate live cells during gelation. 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) 🔴 Gelation triggers 凝膠化觸發: chemical crosslinking 化學交聯, UV, temperature, ionic (Ca²⁺ for alginate 藻酸鹽鈣交聯) 🔴 Advantages: cells encapsulated in true 3D, cytocompatible gelation possible 細胞兼容性凝膠化 🔴 GelMA, PEG, alginate most common 🔴 Photoencapsulation 光封裝 allows spatial patterning 空間圖案化 🔴 Major limitation inside thick hydrogels: diffusion limit 擴散限制 → necrosis 壞死 in centre after ~200–300 μm
Topic 5 – Limitations of Conventional Scaffolds 傳統支架嘅局限性大圖景 (Big Picture)Traditional methods cannot give precise internal channels or full interconnectivity → poor vascularisation in thick constructs. 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) (memorise the list) Poor control of pore size/geometry/spatial distribution 孔徑/幾何形狀/空間分佈 Lack of predefined internal channels 預定義內部通道 Incomplete porogen leaching 不完全嘅致孔劑浸出 in thick scaffolds Closed pores 閉孔 in gas foaming (10–30% interconnected) Residual toxic solvents 殘留有毒溶劑 Cell seeding inefficient 細胞接種效率低 (cells stay on surface, <500 μm penetration)
Topic 6 – Bioreactors in Tissue Engineering 組織工程中嘅生物反應器大圖景 (Big Picture)Static culture fails → bioreactors provide nutrient flow 營養流 & mechanical signals 機械信號. 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) 🔴 Purposes: mass transport 物質傳輸, chemical signals 化學信號, mechanical conditioning 機械調節 🔴 Types: spinner flask 旋轉瓶, rotating wall vessel 旋轉壁容器, perfusion cartridges 灌注盒 🔴 Perfusion 灌注 → uniform cell/matrix distribution throughout scaffold 🟢 Mechanical loading 機械負荷 examples: Cyclic compression 週期性壓縮 → ↑ GAG 糖胺聚糖/collagen in cartilage Stretch 拉伸 → aligned collagen & tenascin 腱生蛋白 in ligament
對比表 (Comparison Table)
Culture Method 培養方法 Cell distribution 細胞分佈 Mass transport 物質傳輸 Mechanical signals 機械信號
Static 靜態 Surface only 僅表面 Diffusion 擴散 None 無
Spinner flask 旋轉瓶 Better 較好 Convection 對流 Minimal 極小
Perfusion 灌注 Homogeneous 均勻 Excellent 優秀 Flow/shear 流動/剪切
Rotating vessel 旋轉容器 Good 良好 Good 良好 Low shear 低剪切
Topic 7 – Host Response to Implanted Biomaterials 宿主對植入生物材料嘅反應大圖景 (Big Picture)Injury → hemostasis 止血 → inflammation 炎症 → granulation 肉芽組織形成 → foreign body reaction 異物反應 → fibrosis/encapsulation 纖維化/包裹. 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) 🔴 Sequence: Hemostasis 止血 → Acute inflammation 急性炎症 (neutrophils 中性粒細胞) → Chronic inflammation 慢性炎症 (macrophages 巨噬細胞) → Granulation tissue 肉芽組織 → Foreign body giant cells 異物巨細胞 → Fibrous capsule 纖維囊 🔴 Biomaterials cause non-specific inflammation 非特異性炎症, NOT immune rejection 非免疫排斥 🔴 Final usual outcome = fibrous encapsulation 纖維包裹 (acellular collagen capsule) 🔴 Capsule thicker with: motion 移動, sharp edges 銳邊, leaching monomers 單體浸出, corrosion 腐蝕
Topic 8 – Immunoisolation & Encapsulated Cell Therapy 免疫隔離與封裝細胞療法大圖景 (Big Picture)Physical barrier protects allogeneic/xenogeneic cells 同種異體/異種細胞 from immune attack while allowing nutrient/waste/hormone exchange. 必須掌握嘅要點 (Must-Know Facts) 🔴 Classic example: alginate-polylysine microcapsules 藻酸鹽-聚賴氨酸微膠囊 for pancreatic islets 胰島 🔴 Membrane MWCO (Molecular Weight Cut-Off) 分子量截留值 typically 50–150 kDa (lets insulin/glucose through, blocks antibodies) 🔴 Allows use of non-autologous cells 非自體細胞 without immunosuppression 無需免疫抑制
Active Recall Bank 主動回憶庫 Name 3 porosity methods that avoid organic solvents 三種避免使用有機溶劑嘅造孔方法 Which method gives closed pores if used alone? 邊種方法單獨使用會產生閉孔？ What is the main advantage of decellularized organs? 脫細胞器官嘅主要優勢係咩？ Why do cells die in the centre of thick hydrogels? 點解厚水凝膠中心嘅細胞會死亡？ Name the sequence of host response stages (6) 列出宿主反應階段嘅順序（6個） What cells dominate acute vs chronic inflammation? 急性 vs 慢性炎症中主導嘅細胞係咩？ What is the usual final fate of a non-degradable implant? 不可降解植入物通常嘅最終命運係咩？ Best bioreactor for uniform cell seeding in thick scaffolds? 用於厚支架均勻接種細胞嘅最佳生物反應器？ Two mechanical signals used in bioreactors for cartilage? 用於軟骨嘅生物反應器中嘅兩種機械信號？ Material of choice for soft vs hard tissue scaffolds? 軟組織 vs 硬組織支架嘅材料選擇？ Pore size range generally optimal? 通常最佳嘅孔徑範圍？ Name one natural hydrogel polymer used for encapsulation 一種用於封裝嘅天然水凝膠聚合物 What does “foreign body giant cell” indicate? “異物巨細胞”表明咩？ Why is perfusion better than static culture? 點解灌注培養好過靜態培養？ Example of self-organized vascularised tissue from iPSCs? 來源於iPSC嘅自組織血管化組織例子？ What does SCPL stand for and what porogen is commonly used? SCPL代表咩？常用咩致孔劑？ Which fabrication method is best for incorporating growth factors without denaturation? 邊種製備方法最適合加入生長因子而不使其變性？ What is the main drawback of melt molding? 熔融成型嘅主要缺點係咩？ What does Re < 2000 mean in microfluidics? 微流體中 Re < 2000 代表咩？ Classic encapsulated cell therapy target disease? 經典嘅封裝細胞療法目標疾病？
(Answers at bottom – cover first! 答案在底部 – 先遮住!) 15-Minute Mock Exam 15分鐘模擬考試 Compare advantages and disadvantages of solvent-casting particulate leaching versus gas foaming/particulate leaching combination. (8 marks)
SCPL 溶劑鑄造粒子浸出: excellent interconnectivity 互連性優秀, good pore control 孔徑控制良好, but uses toxic organic solvent 但使用有毒有機溶劑. Gas foaming + PL 氣體發泡+粒子浸出: no solvent (more biocompatible) 無溶劑 (更生物相容), excellent interconnectivity 互連性優秀, retains temperature-sensitive molecules 保留溫度敏感分子, slightly more complex process 過程稍複雜.
Explain why conventional scaffold techniques fail to produce clinically relevant thick tissues (>1 cm). Give two biological reasons and one fabrication reason. (6 marks)
Diffusion limitation 擴散限制 of oxygen/nutrients (<200–300 μm), lack of pre-formed vascular channels 預成型血管通道, poor cell penetration 細胞滲透 during seeding (cells remain on surface 細胞停留在表面).
Describe the host response timeline from implantation to fibrous encapsulation. (8 marks)
Injury 損傷 → hemostasis & platelet activation 止血 & 血小板激活 → acute inflammation 急性炎症 (neutrophils 中性粒細胞) → chronic inflammation 慢性炎症 (macrophages/monocytes 巨噬細胞/單核細胞) → granulation tissue 肉芽組織 + angiogenesis 血管生成 → foreign body giant cells 異物巨細胞 → fibrous capsule formation 纖維囊形成 (weeks–months 數週-數月).
A company wants to implant insulin-producing cells from pig into diabetic patients without immunosuppression. Suggest and justify a tissue-engineering strategy. (6 marks)
Encapsulate 封裝 porcine islets 豬胰島 in alginate-polylysine microcapsules 藻酸鹽-聚賴氨酸微膠囊 (immunoisolation 免疫隔離). Membrane permits glucose/insulin diffusion 葡萄糖/胰島素擴散 but excludes immune cells/antibodies 免疫細胞/抗體 → prevents rejection 防止排斥 while allowing function.
State four essential requirements of a tissue engineering bioreactor. (4 marks)
Controlled temperature/pH 受控溫度/pH, efficient mass transfer 質量傳遞, delivery of chemical signals 化學信號, ability to apply mechanical/electrical signals 機械/電信號.
One-Page Cheat Sheet Summary 一頁速查表總結
Category 類別 Key Methods / Materials 關鍵方法/材料 Pros 優點 Cons 缺點 Best for 最適用於
Classic porosity 傳統造孔 SCPL, Gas foaming+PL, Freeze-drying High interconnectivity 高互連性 Solvent (SCPL) or heat 溶劑或高溫 General scaffolds 通用支架
Advanced 先進 Electrospinning, 3D printing, Decellularization Precise geometry 精確幾何, native vessels 原生血管 Slow 慢, expensive 貴 Vascularised organs 血管化器官
Cell-friendly 細胞友好 Hydrogels (PEG, GelMA, alginate) Encapsulation during gelation 凝膠過程中封裝 Diffusion limit 擴散限制 3D culture 3D培養, islets 胰島
Cultivation 培養 Perfusion bioreactors 灌注生物反應器 Uniform cell/matrix 均勻細胞/基質 Complex setup 設置複雜 Thick constructs 厚構建體
Host response endpoint 宿主反應終點 Fibrous encapsulation 纖維包裹 Isolates implant 隔離植入物 Avascular wall 無血管壁, no integration 無整合 Most permanent implants 大多數永久植入物
Immunoisolation 免疫隔離 Alginate microcapsules 藻酸鹽微膠囊 No immunosuppression 無需免疫抑制 Fibrosis of capsules over time 膠囊隨時間纖維化 Xenogeneic cells 異種細胞
Active Recall Answers 主動回憶答案:
Gas foaming(+PL) 氣體發泡(+粒子浸出), melt molding 熔融成型, freeze-drying 冷凍乾燥 Gas foaming alone 單獨氣體發泡 Native vascular network retained 保留原生血管網絡 Diffusion limitation 擴散限制 Hemostasis 止血–acute 急性–chronic 慢性–granulation 肉芽組織–giant cells 巨細胞–capsule 囊 Neutrophils 中性粒細胞 vs macrophages 巨噬細胞 Fibrous encapsulation 纖維包裹 Perfusion 灌注 Cyclic compression 週期性壓縮, hydrostatic pressure 靜水壓 Polymers 聚合物 vs ceramics/metals 陶瓷/金屬 100–500 μm Alginate 藻酸鹽/collagen 膠原蛋白 Persistent material or wear particles 持續存在嘅材料或磨損顆粒 Overcomes diffusion limit & uniform seeding 克服擴散限制 & 均勻接種 Liver buds 肝芽 (Takebe 2013) Solvent-casting particulate leaching 溶劑鑄造粒子浸出, NaCl/sugar 氯化鈉/糖 Freeze-drying 冷凍乾燥 or phase separation 相分離 High temperature denatures proteins 高溫使蛋白質變性 Laminar flow 層流 Diabetes (pancreatic islets) 糖尿病 (胰島)
English Version BelowTopic 1 – Scaffold Fabrication Principles & Material SelectionBig Picture (1 sentence + mind map in text)Scaffold must mimic native ECM mechanically and biologically → material choice dictates fabrication method → porosity & interconnectivity are critical for cell migration/nutrient diffusion. Material → Soft tissue (polymers) vs Hard tissue (ceramics/metals/composites) ↓ Fabrication method → Porosity technique ↓ Final scaffold properties
Must-Know Facts (max 15 bullet points) 🔴 Soft tissues (skin, tendon, liver, vessels) → synthetic/natural polymers 🔴 Hard tissues (bone) → metals, ceramics, polymer-containing composites 🔴 Scaffold must have high porosity (>90% ideal), large surface area, interconnected pores 🔴 Pore size: 100–500 μm generally optimal for most tissues 🟢 Fabrication technique depends on polymer solubility, Tg/Tm, biocompatibility needs 🔴 All methods aim to create 3D porous structure for cell infiltration & vascularisation
Key Formulas / Diagrams (use code blocks)Porosity (%) = [1 – (ρscaffold / ρbulk polymer)] × 100
Comparison Table
Property Soft Tissue Scaffolds Hard Tissue Scaffolds
Main materials PLGA, PLLA, collagen, alginate HA, β-TCP, Ti, composites
Mechanical need Elastic, flexible High compressive strength
Typical fabrication Polymer-based (foaming, electrospinning) Ceramic sintering, metal mesh
Common Mistakes & Examiner Traps Forgetting that ceramics alone are too brittle for load-bearing soft tissue Thinking metals are always used for soft tissue (they aren’t)
Mnemonics / Memory Hooks“Soft = Polymer, Hard = Pottery/Metal” (pottery → ceramics) Topic 2 – Conventional Porosity TechniquesBig PictureFive classic ways to make pores; each has trade-offs in interconnectivity, solvent use, and pore control. Must-Know Facts 🔴 Solvent-casting + particulate leaching (SCPL) – gold standard, uses salt/sugar, highly interconnected 🔴 Gas foaming – no organic solvent (advantage), but mostly closed pores 🔴 Gas foaming + particulate leaching – solves interconnectivity problem 🔴 Melt molding – no solvent, uses heat + porogen (e.g., gelatin particles) 🔴 Phase separation – low temperature, good for incorporating growth factors 🔴 Freeze-drying – emulsion or non-emulsion, pore size controlled by freezing rate/pH
Key DiagramsSCPL Process:Polymer + salt in solvent → cast → evaporate solvent → leach salt with water → porous scaffold
Comparison Table
Method Solvent? Interconnected pores? Pore control Bioactive molecule friendly?
SCPL Yes Excellent Good No (solvent)
Gas foaming No Poor (closed) Poor Yes
Gas foaming + PL No Excellent Good Yes
Melt molding No Good Good No (high T)
Phase separation Yes Excellent Good Yes (low T)
Freeze-drying Yes/No Excellent Excellent Yes
Common Mistakes Saying gas foaming alone gives high interconnectivity (it doesn’t) Forgetting polymer must be insoluble in water but porogen must be water-soluble
Topic 3 – Advanced Biofabrication TechniquesBig PictureMove from random porosity to precise, reproducible 3D architecture + ability to include cells during printing. Must-Know Facts 🔴 Electrospinning → nanofibers, excellent for ECM mimicry, cheap & fast 🔴 Rapid prototyping / 3D printing / microstereolithography → CAD-CAM control of exact geometry 🔴 Microsphere sintering → trabecular bone-like pores 🔴 Decellularized organs → keep native vascular network (Ott 2008, 2010 – perfused lung) 🟢 Decellularization + recellularization = whole organ engineering gold standard
Comparison Table
Technique Resolution Speed Cell inclusion during fab? Vascular network?
Electrospinning Nano Fast No No
3D Bioprinting 50–100 μm Slow Yes (bioink) Possible
Decellularization Native Slow No (recell later) Yes (native)
Examiner TrapStudents forget decellularized scaffolds already have perfect vascular tree Topic 4 – Hydrogels & Cell EncapsulationBig PictureHydrogels = water-swollen polymer networks; unique ability to encapsulate live cells during gelation. Must-Know Facts 🔴 Gelation triggers: chemical crosslinking, UV, temperature, ionic (Ca²⁺ for alginate) 🔴 Advantages: cells encapsulated in true 3D, cytocompatible gelation possible 🔴 GelMA, PEG, alginate most common 🔴 Photoencapsulation allows spatial patterning 🔴 Major limitation inside thick hydrogels: diffusion limit → necrosis in centre after ~200–300 μm
Mnemonics“Hydrogels = Hotel for cells” – cells check in alive during crosslinking Topic 5 – Limitations of Conventional ScaffoldsBig PictureTraditional methods cannot give precise internal channels or full interconnectivity → poor vascularisation in thick constructs. Must-Know Facts (memorise the list) Poor control of pore size/geometry/spatial distribution Lack of predefined internal channels Incomplete porogen leaching in thick scaffolds Closed pores in gas foaming (10–30% interconnected) Residual toxic solvents Cell seeding inefficient (cells stay on surface, <500 μm penetration)
Topic 6 – Bioreactors in Tissue EngineeringBig PictureStatic culture fails → bioreactors provide nutrient flow & mechanical signals. Must-Know Facts 🔴 Purposes: mass transport, chemical signals, mechanical conditioning 🔴 Types: spinner flask, rotating wall vessel, perfusion cartridges 🔴 Perfusion → uniform cell/matrix distribution throughout scaffold 🟢 Mechanical loading examples: Cyclic compression → ↑ GAG/collagen in cartilage Stretch → aligned collagen & tenascin in ligament
Comparison Table
Culture Method Cell distribution Mass transport Mechanical signals
Static Surface only Diffusion None
Spinner flask Better Convection Minimal
Perfusion Homogeneous Excellent Flow/shear
Rotating vessel Good Good Low shear
Topic 7 – Host Response to Implanted BiomaterialsBig PictureInjury → hemostasis → inflammation → granulation → foreign body reaction → fibrosis/encapsulation. Must-Know Facts 🔴 Sequence: Hemostasis → Acute inflammation (neutrophils) → Chronic inflammation (macrophages) → Granulation tissue → Foreign body giant cells → Fibrous capsule 🔴 Biomaterials cause non-specific inflammation, NOT immune rejection 🔴 Final usual outcome = fibrous encapsulation (acellular collagen capsule) 🔴 Capsule thicker with: motion, sharp edges, leaching monomers, corrosion
Mnemonics“Hemostasis → Acute → Chronic → Granulation → Giant cells → Capsule” = HAC3G Topic 8 – Immunoisolation & Encapsulated Cell TherapyBig PicturePhysical barrier protects allogeneic/xenogeneic cells from immune attack while allowing nutrient/waste/hormone exchange. Must-Know Facts 🔴 Classic example: alginate-polylysine microcapsules for pancreatic islets 🔴 Membrane MWCO typically 50–150 kDa (lets insulin/glucose through, blocks antibodies) 🔴 Allows use of non-autologous cells without immunosuppression
Active Recall Bank Name 3 porosity methods that avoid organic solvents Which method gives closed pores if used alone? What is the main advantage of decellularized organs? Why do cells die in the centre of thick hydrogels? Name the sequence of host response stages (6) What cells dominate acute vs chronic inflammation? What is the usual final fate of a non-degradable implant? Best bioreactor for uniform cell seeding in thick scaffolds? Two mechanical signals used in bioreactors for cartilage? Material of choice for soft vs hard tissue scaffolds? Pore size range generally optimal? Name one natural hydrogel polymer used for encapsulation What does “foreign body giant cell” indicate? Why is perfusion better than static culture? Example of self-organized vascularised tissue from iPSCs? What does SCPL stand for and what porogen is commonly used? Which fabrication method is best for incorporating growth factors without denaturation? What is the main drawback of melt molding? What does Re < 2000 mean in microfluidics? Classic encapsulated cell therapy target disease?
(Answers at bottom – cover first!) 15-Minute Mock Exam Compare advantages and disadvantages of solvent-casting particulate leaching versus gas foaming/particulate leaching combination. (8 marks)
SCPL: excellent interconnectivity, good pore control, but uses toxic organic solvent. Gas foaming + PL: no solvent (more biocompatible), excellent interconnectivity, retains temperature-sensitive molecules, slightly more complex process.
Explain why conventional scaffold techniques fail to produce clinically relevant thick tissues (>1 cm). Give two biological reasons and one fabrication reason. (6 marks)
Diffusion limitation of oxygen/nutrients (<200–300 μm), lack of pre-formed vascular channels, poor cell penetration during seeding (cells remain on surface).
Describe the host response timeline from implantation to fibrous encapsulation. (8 marks)
Injury → hemostasis & platelet activation → acute inflammation (neutrophils) → chronic inflammation (macrophages/monocytes) → granulation tissue + angiogenesis → foreign body giant cells → fibrous capsule formation (weeks–months).
A company wants to implant insulin-producing cells from pig into diabetic patients without immunosuppression. Suggest and justify a tissue-engineering strategy. (6 marks)
Encapsulate porcine islets in alginate-polylysine microcapsules (immunoisolation). Membrane permits glucose/insulin diffusion but excludes immune cells/antibodies → prevents rejection while allowing function.
State four essential requirements of a tissue engineering bioreactor. (4 marks)
Controlled temperature/pH, efficient mass transfer, delivery of chemical signals, ability to apply mechanical/electrical signals.
One-Page Cheat Sheet Summary
Category Key Methods / Materials Pros Cons Best for
Classic porosity SCPL, Gas foaming+PL, Freeze-drying High interconnectivity Solvent (SCPL) or heat General scaffolds
Advanced Electrospinning, 3D printing, Decellularization Precise geometry, native vessels Slow, expensive Vascularised organs
Cell-friendly Hydrogels (PEG, GelMA, alginate) Encapsulation during gelation Diffusion limit 3D culture, islets
Cultivation Perfusion bioreactors Uniform cell/matrix Complex setup Thick constructs
Host response endpoint Fibrous encapsulation Isolates implant Avascular wall, no integration Most permanent implants
Immunoisolation Alginate microcapsules No immunosuppression Fibrosis of capsules over time Xenogeneic cells
Active Recall Answers: 1. Gas foaming(+PL), melt molding, freeze-drying 2. Gas foaming alone 3. Native vascular network retained 4. Diffusion limitation 5. Hemostasis–acute–chronic–granulation–giant cells–capsule 6. Neutrophils vs macrophages 7. Fibrous encapsulation 8. Perfusion 9. Cyclic compression, hydrostatic pressure 10. Polymers vs ceramics/metals 11. 100–500 μm 12. Alginate/collagen 13. Persistent material or wear particles 14. Overcomes diffusion limit & uniform seeding 15. Liver buds (Takebe 2013) 16. Solvent-casting particulate leaching, NaCl/sugar 17. Freeze-drying or phase separation 18. High temperature denatures proteins 19. Laminar flow 20. Diabetes (pancreatic islets)

entry.title: HW1 Signals and Systems | 生物信號與生物系統
entry.content:
Homework 1In this homework set, you will work with a set of Visual Evoked Response (VER) data. The VER is an EEG signal recorded near the visual cortex following a visual stimulus, like a flash of light. The individual recordings contain a significant amount of noise, thus we will be using ensemble averaging to reduce the noise and enhance the signal response.
這份作業的目的是讓你熟悉如何處理視覺誘發反應（VER）腦電訊號，並透過**集成平均（ensemble averaging）**來降低噪聲、提取神經反應訊號。VER 是在視覺皮質區域記錄的 EEG 訊號，通常在視覺刺激（如閃光）後出現。由於單次試驗的訊號中含有大量噪聲，因此我們需要透過多次試驗的平均來強化真正的神經反應。
🧠 Part 1: Plot EEG signal 繪製原始 EEG 訊號✅ Objective: Visualize one raw EEG signal to understand its noisy nature. 觀察單次試驗的腦電訊號，了解其噪聲特性。📌 Steps:
Load the VER data from ver.mat. This contains 100 simulated VERs. 從 ver.mat 檔案中載入 VER 資料。
Check the size of ver. 檢查 ver 的大小（應為 100 × 500）。
Plot one of the individual responses to visualize the “raw” EEG signal. 繪製第一筆試驗資料（即 ver 的第一列）。
load('ver.mat'); % Load VER datadisp(size(ver)); % Display the size of the matrixt = 0:0.005:(size(ver,2)-1)*0.005; % Create time vector based on sampling intervalfigure;plot(t, ver(1,:)); % Plot the first trialxlabel('Time (s)');ylabel('EEG Amplitude');title('Raw VER Signal (Trial 1)'); 🔍 Code Breakdown*t = 0:0.005:(size(ver,2)-1)0.005;
size(ver,2) gets the number of columns in the matrix ver, which represents the number of time points per trial.
0:0.005:… creates a vector starting at 0, with increments of 0.005 seconds (sampling interval).
The final value is (size(ver,2)-1)*0.005, which ensures the time vector matches the length of each trial.This time vector t is essential for plotting EEG signals against time in seconds.
size(ver,2) 取得 ver 矩陣的欄數，也就是每筆試驗的時間點數量。
0:0.005:… 表示從 0 開始，每 0.005 秒取一個時間點（即採樣間隔）。
結尾的 (size(ver,2)-1)*0.005 確保時間向量的長度與試驗資料一致。這樣建立的 t 向量可以用來在繪圖時將腦電訊號對應到實際時間（秒）。
📊 Part 2: Ensemble Averaging 集成平均✅ Objective: Reduce noise by averaging multiple trials. 透過多次試驗平均來降低噪聲。📌 Steps:
Use MATLAB’s mean function to average multiple trials. 使用 MATLAB 的 mean 函數來平均多條試驗訊號。 First, average the first 25 trials → call this avg25. 先平均前 25 條試驗 → 命名為 avg25 Then, average all 100 trials → call this avg100. 再平均全部 100 條試驗 → 命名為 avg100 Plot: One raw trial avg25 avg100 All on the same graph to compare how averaging reduces noise. 看看平均後雜訊如何減少。
avg25 = mean(ver(1:25,:), 1); % Average of first 25 trialsavg100 = mean(ver, 1); % Average of all 100 trialsfigure;plot(t, ver(1,:), 'Color',[0.7,0.7,0.7]); hold on;plot(t, avg25, 'b', 'LineWidth', 1.5);plot(t, avg100, 'r', 'LineWidth', 1.5);xlabel('Time (s)');ylabel('EEG Amplitude');legend('Raw Trial', '25-Trial Avg', '100-Trial Avg');title('VER Signal Averaging Comparison'); 🔍 Code Breakdownavg25 = mean(ver(1:25,:), 1);
ver is a matrix or 2D array. 是一個矩陣或二維陣列。 1:25 means you’re selecting rows 1 through 25. 表示你正在選取第 1 到第 25 列的資料。 ****: means you’re selecting all columns in those rows. 表示你選取這些列中的所有欄位。So, ver(1:25,:) gives you a 25×N matrix, where N is the number of columns in ver.所以 ver(1:25,:) 會回傳一個 25×N 的矩陣，其中 N 是 ver 的欄位數（也就是每筆試驗的時間點數量）。 mean(…, 1) Computes the mean along the first dimension (rows), meaning it averages the data across the 25 trials for each time point. 對這 25 行資料沿著第 1 維（行）做平均，也就是對每一個時間點的 25 次試驗取平均。 avg25: Stores the result of the averaging, which is a 1×N vector representing the average EEG signal at each time point. 儲存平均後的結果，是一個1×N 的向量，代表每個時間點的平均 EEG 訊號。
🔍 Part III: Noise Reduction Evaluation 噪聲降低評估✅ Objective: Quantify how much noise is reduced by averaging. 量化平均處理後的噪聲降低效果。📌 Steps:
Load actual_ver from ver.mat (the noise-free signal). 從 ver.mat 中載入無噪聲的 VER 訊（actual_ver）。 Subtract actual_ver from: 分別從原始試驗、avg25 和 avg100 中減去 actual_ver，得到噪聲估計。 One raw trial avg25 avg100 to estimate noise.
Compute standard deviation of each noise estimate: 計算每個噪聲估計的標準差（STD）作為噪聲強度指標。 std_ver, std_avg25, std_avg100
load('ver.mat', 'actual_ver'); % Load noise-free signalnoise_ver = ver(1,:) - actual_ver;noise_avg25 = avg25 - actual_ver;noise_avg100 = avg100 - actual_ver;std_ver = std(noise_ver);std_avg25 = std(noise_avg25);std_avg100 = std(noise_avg100);disp(['STD of individual noise: ', num2str(std_ver)]);disp(['STD of 25-trial average noise: ', num2str(std_avg25)]);disp(['STD of 100-trial average noise: ', num2str(std_avg100)]);
📐 Part IV: Comparison with Theoretical Prediction 與理論預測比較✅Objective: Compare actual noise reduction with theoretical expectations. 驗證噪聲是否依照理論預測隨平均次數減少📌Steps:
Use the formula:-\text{Expected STD} = \frac{\text{STD of raw trial}}{\sqrt{N}}- where N = 25 or 100 Compare expected vs actual standard deviations.- 計算預測值並與實際值比較。 Discuss any differences and possible reasons . 討論差異與可能原因。
expected_std_avg25 = std_ver / sqrt(25);expected_std_avg100 = std_ver / sqrt(100);disp(['Expected STD for 25-trial: ', num2str(expected_std_avg25)]);disp(['Expected STD for 100-trial: ', num2str(expected_std_avg100)]); Result | 結果%code sample for Avg_25fprintf('Actual STD (Avg25): %.4f | Expected: %.4f\n | Differeces: %.4f\n', std_avg25, expected_std_avg25, diff_avg25);
Actual STD (Raw): 1.0405 Actual STD (Avg25): 0.1986 | Expected: 0.2081 | Differeces: 0.0095 Actual STD (Avg100): 0.1005 | Expected: 0.1040 | Differeces: 0.0035
Discussion of Discrepancies | 差異原因探討 :Several factors could explain the slight differences between actual and expected STD values | 儘管差異不大，仍可能由以下因素造成 :
Non-Gaussian Noise: The theoretical model assumes random, Gaussian-distributed noise. Real EEG data often includes artifacts (e.g., muscle movement, eye blinks) that deviate from this assumption.
非高斯噪音：理論假設噪音為高斯分布，但實際 EEG 資料可能包含肌肉活動、眼動等非隨機干擾。
Signal Variability Across Trials: If the underlying neural response varies slightly between trials, averaging won’t perfectly cancel out the variability, leading to a higher actual STD than expected.
試驗間訊號變異：若神經反應在不同試驗中略有差異，平均後仍可能保留部分變異。
Baseline Drift or Slow Trends: Low-frequency fluctuations in the signal can persist across trials and resist averaging, subtly inflating the STD.
基線漂移或慢速趨勢：低頻波動可能在多次試驗中持續存在，不易被平均消除。
Finite Sample Effects: The expected STD is based on ideal statistical behavior. With only 25 or 100 trials, small sample effects can cause minor deviations from theory.
樣本數有限：理論值基於理想統計行為，實際試驗數量有限時可能出現微小偏差。
Filtering or Preprocessing: If any filtering was applied before averaging, it could alter the noise characteristics and affect STD calculations
前處理影響：若在平均前進行過濾或其他處理，可能改變噪音特性，影響 STD 計算。

entry.title: HW2 Signals and Systems | 生物信號與生物系統
entry.content:
Homework 2In this homework set, you will aims to build your skills in visualizing signal transformations and understanding the process of discretization, which are fundamental in analyzing and processing biomedical signals in systems like those used in medical diagnostics or monitoring.
在這組作業中，您將致力於培養可視化訊號轉換和理解離散化過程的技能，這些技能對於分析和處理醫療診斷或監測系統中的生物醫學訊號至關重要。
Code usage in MatlabTo obtain the orginal “Continous Time Signal with Straight Line” x(t)
x-axis represent: Time y-axis represent: Amplitude
y = [0,0,-5,5,-3,2,1,0,0];t = [-1,0,0,1,2,3,4,5,6];figure;subplot(3,2,1);plot( t, y, 'b', 'LineWidth', 1.5); xlabel('Time');ylabel('Amplitude');title('Continous Time Signal with Straight Line');grid on;
Signal Transformations | 訊號轉換: Question 1 and 2
Draw 𝑥(𝑡+1).
Draw 𝑥(−2𝑡+1).
Question 1Question 2Question 1Draw Draw 𝑥(𝑡+1):
t+1 mean moved the signal toward left by 1 unit | 將信號向左移動 1 單位 Or by calculate | 或透過計算 : orginal_time_data = new_time_data + 1 new_time_data = orginal_time_data - 1 [ -1, 0, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6] -> [ -2, -1, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
t_q1 = t-1; % by using the fomular abovesubplot(3,2,2);plot( t_q1, y, 'b', 'LineWidth', 1.5); xlabel('Time');ylabel('Amplitude');title('*Question 1');grid on;Question 2Draw Draw 𝑥(-2𝑡+1):
In −2𝑡+1 involve: “2t” time scaling | 時間縮放: compression by a factor of 2. | 壓縮 2 倍。 “-“reversal | 反轉: due to the negative sign - infront of 2t. | 由於 2t 前面的負號 -。 “+1” shifting | 移位 : moved the signal. | 訊號平移。
By calculate | 或透過計算 : orginal_time_data = 2 * new_time_data + 1 new_time_data = - (orginal_time_data - 1) / 2 [ -1, 0, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6] -> [ 1, 0.5, 0.5, 0, -0.5, -1, -1.5, -2, -2.5]
t_q2 = -(t-1)/2; % by using the fomular abovesubplot(3,2,3);plot(t_q2, y, 'b', 'LineWidth', 1.5); xlabel('Time');ylabel('Amplitude');title('Question 2');grid on;
Signal Sampling : Question 3, 4 & 5
Discretize 𝑥(𝑡) using a sampling period of 𝑇𝑠=1.
Discretize 𝑥(𝑡) using a sampling period of 𝑇𝑠=2, starting from t = 0.
Discretize 𝑥(𝑡) using a sampling period of 𝑇𝑠=2, starting from t = -1.
Discretize 𝑥(𝑡) | 離散化𝑥(𝑡): We need to extract the most significant (extreme) value — either the maximum or minimum — within each 1-second interval. | 我們需要提取每 1 秒間隔內最重要的（極端）值 - 最大值或最小值。From the original graph we can obtain following result of the Discretize dataset:
discretize_y = [0,-5,5,-3,2,1,0,0];discretize_t = [-1,0,1,2,3,4,5,6];
Question 3Question 4Question 2Question 3Discretize 𝑥(𝑡) using a sampling period of 𝑇𝑠=1.:
Discretize 𝑥(𝑡): This introduces the concept of sampling a continuous signal at regular intervals (every 1 unit of time) | 以固定間隔（每 1 個時間單位）對連續訊號進行取樣的概念
subplot(3,2,4);stem( discretize_t, discretize_y, 'b', 'LineWidth', 1.5); xlabel('Time (s)');ylabel('Amplitude');title('3. Discretize 𝑥(𝑡) using a sampling period of 𝑇𝑠=1.')grid on;Question 4Discretize 𝑥(𝑡) using a sampling period of 𝑇𝑠=2, starting from t = 0 :
Discretize 𝑥(𝑡): similarly to question 3
Sampling period of 𝑇𝑠=2, starting from t = 0: Sampling with a larger interval and start from time = 0 | 以更大的間隔採樣，並從時間= 0開始 In this situation we use list_name(starting_index:inverval:end) to obtain the data In Matlab index starting with [1,2,3,4] not [0,1,2,3] | 在Matlab中，索引以**[1,2,3,4]開頭，而非[0,1,2,3]**
discretize_y(2:2:end) = [0,-5,5,-3,2,1,0,0] -> [-5,-3,1,0] discretize_t(2:2:end) = [-1,0,1,2,3,4,5,6] -> [0,2,4,6]
discretize_t_q4 = discretize_t(2:2:end); discretize_y_q4 = discretize_y(2:2:end); subplot(3,2,5);stem(discretize_t_q4, discretize_y_q4, 'b','LineWidth', 1.5);xlabel('Time');ylabel('Amplitude');title('4. Discretize 𝑥(𝑡) using a sampling period of 𝑇𝑠=2, starting from t = 0 ');grid on;Question 55. Discretize 𝑥(𝑡) using a sampling period of 𝑇𝑠=2, starting from t = -1 :
Discretize 𝑥(𝑡): similarly to question 3
Sampling period of 𝑇𝑠=2, starting from t = -1 : Sampling with a larger interval and start from time = -1 | | 以更大的間隔採樣，並從時間= -1開始 In this situation we use list_name(starting_index:inverval:end) to obtain the data In Matlab index starting with [1,2,3,4] not [0,1,2,3] | 在Matlab中，索引以**[1,2,3,4]開頭，而非[0,1,2,3]**
discretize_y(1:2:end) = [0,-5,5,-3,2,1,0,0] -> [0,5,2,0] discretize_t(1:2:end) = [-1,0,1,2,3,4,5,6] -> [-1,1,3,5]
discretize_t_q5 = discretize_t(1:2:end); discretize_y_q5 = discretize_y(1:2:end);subplot(3,2,6);stem( discretize_t_q5, discretize_y_q5, 'b', 'LineWidth', 1.5);xlabel('Time');ylabel('Amplitude');title('5. Discretize 𝑥(𝑡) using a sampling period of 𝑇𝑠=2, starting from t = -1');grid on;
Result output | 結果輸出% Display output with windowsset(gcf, 'Position', [100, 100, 800, 600]);
gcf: “Get Current Figure” — refers to the active figure window | 取得活動圖形視窗 ‘Position’: Specifies the window’s position and size | 指定視窗的位置和大小 [100, 100, 800, 600]: 100, 100: Position of the bottom-left corner of the window on your screen (in pixels) | 視窗左下角在螢幕上的位置（以像素為單位） 800: Width of the window | 視窗寬度 600: Height of the window | 視窗高度
